# Partner Manager - Full Documentation

# Accessing Source Message Attributes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

When you create translation or reference maps for inbound or outbound message flows, you can access
source message attributes in those maps. You can then map the attributes to fields in the translated
message payload or use the attribute values in your mapping logic to perform dynamic condition
execution.

## Common Use Cases

Accessing source message attributes helps in many use cases, such as with performing dynamic message
routing decisions, performing specific functions, and mapping fields within the data transformation
in the message flow execution.

Some common use cases include:

- Achieving end-to-end transmission tracing

  Accessing transmission IDs in the translation maps enables end-to-end transmission tracing because
  these IDs are passed to the translated JSON or XML output file.

- Mapping attributes to pass them to the backend

  For example, if the backend must see the source filename or a specific HTTP header, you can access
  the filename or HTTP header and map the values to the translated message payload fields.

- Applying mapping logic based either on a filename part or pattern within the transformation

  For example, you can access a filename prefix through substring operations and then build dynamic
  mapping logic based on this prefix.

- Mapping attributes to make them available in the search

  For example, you can access the source filename or a specific HTTP header value as a custom
  message attribute in the message typeâs reference map to easily search for these values in the
  Activity screens.

For more information, see [Use Case Implementation Examples](#implementation-examples).

The source attributes that you can access are available within a predefined variable named
`b2bMessageContext`. The content of this variable differs depending on the source endpointâs
protocol.

## AS2 Source Endpoint

Messages received via AS2 contain the following fields in the `b2bMessageContext` variable:

- sourceProtocol
- transmissionId
- sourceEndpointAttributes:
  - as2MessageId
  - as2From
  - as2To
  - fileName
  - subject
  - fileSize

- customProtocolHeaders

  For security reasons, only the headers with the prefix `x-apm-` are available in the
  `b2bMessageContext` variable.

For example:

```json
{
  "b2bMessageContext": {
    "sourceProtocol": "AS2",
    "transmissionId": "06bc20c8-475e-4a6f-b010-9b7933b2bfac",
    "sourceEndpointAttributes": {
      "as2MessageId": "<2023-04-12T19:37:42.273-46b95696-83f4-4e65-9222-61c40a854200@partnera_mythical-as2>",
      "as2From": "partnera",
      "as2To": "mythical-as2",
      "fileName": "EDI_850_20230401_1245678.edi",
      "subject": "EDI Message via Mule Generic Service",
      "fileSize": 3887
    },
    "customProtocolHeaders": {
      "x-apm-order-type": "drop-ship",
      "x-apm-tracking-id": "9d26d4ce-59ea-4448-8b51-ccc975d4efec"
    },
    "messageId": "9bf446f8-4a02-475f-afa3-c8772a340154"
  }
}
```

## FTP and SFTP Source Endpoint

Messages received via FTP or SFTP contain the following fields in the `b2bMessageContext` variable:

- sourceProtocol
- transmissionId
- sourceEndpointAttributes:
  - fileName
  - filePath
  - fileSize
  - fileTimeStamp
  - partnerReferenceId

    This attribute is available only for Receive from Partner endpoints that have the partner
    reference ID configured.

- messageId

For example:

```json
{
  "b2bMessageContext": {
    "sourceProtocol": "SFTP",
    "transmissionId": "3ef5654a-5cda-41b1-8909-59a28c8b1537",
    "sourceEndpointAttributes": {
      "fileName": "RandomMessageType_PO_20230331_1559_003.xml",
      "filePath": "/partners/pacific-corp/in-orders",
      "fileSize": 4889,
      "fileTimeStamp": "2023-04-05T15:16:29",
      "partnerReferenceId": "PACIFICCORP"
    },
    "messageId": "cdaf6c12-bb3b-4978-9ac6-e8ace053f7c5"
  }
}
```

## HTTP and HTTPS Source Endpoint

Messages received via HTTP or HTTPS contain the following fields in the `b2bMessageContext`
variable:

- sourceProtocol
- transmissionId
- sourceEndpointAttributes:
  - path
  - fileSize

- customProtocolHeaders

  For security reasons, only the headers with the prefix `x-apm-` are available in the
  `b2bMessageContext` variable.

- messageId

For example:

```json
{
  "b2bMessageContext": {
    "sourceProtocol": "HTTPS",
    "transmissionId": "a7a800ad-4117-40c1-98a0-79892c982fd4",
    "sourceEndpointAttributes": {
      "path": "/",
      "fileSize": 866
    },
    "customProtocolHeaders": {
      "x-apm-order-number": "ORD12345-A",
      "x-apm-order-type": "drop-ship"
    },
    "messageId": "cdaf6c12-bb3b-4978-9ac6-e8ace053f7c5"
  }
}
```

## Use Case Implementation Examples

The following examples show how to implement some common use cases for source message attributes.

This example shows how to map the `messageId` and `fileName` attributes in the translated data
payload within a message flowâs translation map:

messageId: vars.b2bMessageContext.messageId fileName:
vars.b2bMessageContext.sourceEndpointAttributes.fileName

This example shows how to apply mapping logic based on the filename prefix message in the message
flowâs translation map:

POPurpose: substringBefore(vars.b2bMessageContext.sourceEndpointAttributes.fileName, "\_") default
"PO-CREATE",

This example shows how to perform a `substringBefore` operation on the source filename to get the
value before `_` and use it as the partner reference identifier:

%dw 2.0 output application/json import \* from dw::core::Strings ns ns0
http://xmlns.mulesoft.com/enterpriseobjects/b2b/

---

{ partnerReferenceId: substringBefore(vars.b2bMessageContext.sourceEndpointAttributes.fileName,
"\_") default "UNKNOWN" , hostReferenceId:
payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#Seller, businessDocumentKey:
payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#PurchaseOrderNumber, customAttributes: \[ {
alias: "purchaseOrderNumber", values:
\[payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#PurchaseOrderNumber\] }, { alias:
"buyerEmail", values: \[payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#BuyerEmailID\] } \] }

This example shows how to:

- Use the partner identifier associated with an FTP or SFTP Receive from Partners endpoint
  configuration as the partner reference identifier for routing the message.
- Map the source filename as a custom message attribute.

%dw 2.0 output application/json ns ns0 http://xmlns.mulesoft.com/enterpriseobjects/b2b/

---

{ partnerReferenceId: vars.b2bMessageContext.sourceEndpointAttributes.partnerReferenceId,
hostReferenceId: payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#Seller, businessDocumentKey:
payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#PurchaseOrderNumber, customAttributes: \[ {
alias: "purchaseOrderNumber", values:
\[payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#PurchaseOrderNumber\] }, { alias:
"buyerEmail", values: \[payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#BuyerEmailID\] }, {
alias: "sourceFileName", values: \[vars.b2bMessageContext.sourceEndpointAttributes.fileName default
âUNKNOWN\] } \] }

This example shows how to map a custom header on the received HTTP message as a custom message
attribute:

%dw 2.0 output application/json ns ns0 http://xmlns.mulesoft.com/enterpriseobjects/b2b/

---

{ partnerReferenceId: payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#Seller, hostReferenceId:
payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#Buyer, businessDocumentKey:
payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.ns0#PurchaseOrder.ns0#PurchaseOrderNumber, customAttributes: \[ {
alias: "purchaseOrderNumber", values: payload.ns0#B2BMessage.ns0#Data.\*ns0#PurchaseOrder map (
purchaseOrder , indexOfPurchaseOrder ) -> purchaseOrder.ns0#PurchaseOrderNumber default "UNKNOWN" },
{ alias: "supplierName", values: \[vars.b2bMessageContext.customProtocolHeaders.'x-apm-supplierName'
default "UNKNOWN"\] } \] }

## See Also

- [Translation Maps](partner-manager-maps)
- [Creating Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Reference Maps for Message Types](create-reference-map)
- [Tracking Transmissions](activity-tracking)
---

# Tracking Individual Messages

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The message view in Anypoint Partner Manager provides visibility into individual transactions within
a transmission. Use the message view to monitor the messages exchanged between partner and
suppliers, view the message payload, and see how individual messages are processed and routed.

The message view has one record for each transaction. For example, if an incoming transmission
contains 30 transactions, the message view has 30 corresponding records.

A message record contains the following information:

- Business key and other custom attributes that are included in the message type definition
- Pre-translated and post-translated payloads

Partner Manager does not create message view records when:

- An inbound transaction fails during validation, resulting in a complete rejection of the
  transaction.
- An inbound transaction fails due to AS2 security issues.
- An outbound transaction fails when translating the source payload to a different format.
- An outbound transaction fails when writing X12 or EDIFACT data.

To track transmissions instead of individual messages, see
[Tracking Transmissions](activity-tracking).

## Locate a Message

Select a message to view its details and view and download its contents:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Activity**.
2.  Leave the default, **Messages**.
3.  Search for the message.
4.  Optionally, filter the search results based on the following criteria:
    - Partner name
    - Message direction
    - Date range within which the message was sent

      You can retrieve up to one weekâs worth of messages.

    - Message processing status, such as `Received`
    - Acknowledgement status, such as `Overdue`
    - Partner message type, such as `X12 - 850`
    - Host message type, such as `JSON host-purchase-order`
    - Message attribute, if the message type associated with the message has custom attributes
    - Message value
    - Transmission or message ID

    Partner Manager returns a list of messages that meet your search criteria along with related
    information such as the date the message was received, message status, message type, partner,
    document ID, business key, and acknowledgment status.

5.  Click on a message to review additional information about the message and to view its message
    flow.

## View Additional Details for an Inbound Message

The additional details for an inbound message include the following information:

- Message ID
- Transmission ID
- Message flow name
- Functional acknowledgment name, if one exists
- Information about the message source, including the date and time when the message was received
  from the source, partner message type, endpoint name, filename, and target protocol
- Identifiers and control number associated with the message
- Information about the message target, including the date and time when the message was delivered,
  host message type, target endpoint, protocol, and target filename

Take any of the following actions to view and download additional information about the inbound
message:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>Steps</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View the transmission that contains the message.</p></td><td><p>Click the link in the <strong>Transmission Id</strong> field.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View information about the source (Receive from Partner) endpoint.</p></td><td><div><div><p>In the <strong>Source</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Source endpoint</strong> field.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View information about the target (Target at Host) endpoint.</p></td><td><div><div><p>In the <strong>Target</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Target endpoint</strong> field.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the payload received from the partner.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Source</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Source file</strong> field.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the payload sent to the backend.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Target</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Target file</strong> field.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the MDN message sent to the partner in response to inbound AS2 transmissions.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>Click the link in the <strong>Transmission details</strong> field to access the <strong>Transmission Flow</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Sent MDN</strong> field, click <strong>View MDN</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the EDIFACT CONTRL message or X12 997 acknowledgment message sent to the partner, if one exists.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>Click the link in the <strong>Transmission details</strong> field to access the <strong>Transmission Flow</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Processed message through message flows</strong> section, click <strong>View Control</strong> or <strong>View 997</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View the message flow diagram for the transmitted message.</p></td><td><p>Click the link in the <strong>Processed message through message flows</strong> field.<br><br>Expand the message flow diagram sections to view additional information, including information about the receiving (Receive from Partners) endpoint, source message, DataWeave map, target message type, and target (Target at Host) endpoint.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## View Details for an Outbound Message

The details for an outbound message include the following information:

- Date and time when the file was received from the backend
- Functional acknowledgment, if one exists
- Name of the transmitted file
- Information about the message source, including the source message type, source endpoint name, and
  source protocol
- Identifiers and control numbers associated with the message
- Information about the message target, including the partner message type, date, and time when the
  message was sent to the partner, target filename, and target endpoint name

Take any of the following actions to view and download additional information about the outbound
message:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>Steps</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View and download the payload transmitted to the partner.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Summary</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Transmission details</strong> field.</p></li><li><p>Click the link in the <strong>File</strong> field.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View information about the source (Source at Host) endpoint.</p></td><td><p>In the <strong>Source</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Source endpoint</strong> field.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the payload received from the backend.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Source</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Source file</strong> field.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View information about the target (Send to Partners) endpoint.</p></td><td><p>In the <strong>Target</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Target endpoint</strong> field.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the payload sent to the partner.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Target</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Target file</strong> field.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the MDN received from the partner in response to outbound AS2 transmissions.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Summary</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Transmission details</strong> field to access the message flow diagram.</p></li><li><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Sent to</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Received MDN</strong> field, click <strong>View payload</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View the EDIFACT CONTRL or X12 997 acknowledgment message received from the partner, if one exists.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Summary</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Transmission details</strong> field to access the message flow diagram.</p></li><li><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Sent to</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Sent transmission</strong> field, click <strong>View payload</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the DataWeave map that transformed the transmission.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Summary</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Transmission details</strong> field to access the message flow diagram.</p></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Map</strong> section to expand it.</p></li><li><p>Click the link in this section.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## View Message Summaries

Use AI to generate a summary of the message payload. Make sure youâve enabled Einstein in
[CloudHub 1.0](cloudhub-deployment-settings), [CloudHub 2.0](cloudhub2-deployment-settings), or
[Runtime Fabric](rtf-deployment-settings) deployment settings.

To generate a summary, open the source or target payload on the message details page and click
**Summarize**. If this is the first time youâre viewing a summary, accept the conditions to allow
the use of Einstein.

The summary shows key details, such as the message type, sender, and receiver, as well as the
contents of the message like the order number, customer name, and order details.

## See Also

- [Inbound Message Flows](inbound-message-flows)
- [Outbound Message Flows](outbound-message-flows)
- [EDI Acknowledgment Reconciliation](edi-ack-reconciliation)
- [Troubleshooting Anypoint Partner Manager](troubleshooting)
---

# Tracking Transmissions

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

In Anypoint Partner Manager, one transmission can consist of one or more messages. Use the
transmission view to monitor transmissions exchanged between partners and to view the transmission
contents. At a high level, a transmission record contains the following information:

- Data payloads received from partners or sent to partners via a configured protocol such as AS2,
  HTTP, SFTP or FTP
- Message disposition notifications (MDNs), if the payload is exchanged with partners via AS2
  protocol functional acknowledgments

To track individual messages, see [Tracking Individual Messages](activity-message-tracking).

## Locate a Transmission

Select a transmission to view its details and view and download the message contents:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Activity**.
2.  Select **Transmissions**.
3.  Search for the transmission.
4.  Optionally, filter the search results based on the following criteria:
    - Partner name
    - Message direction
    - Date range within which the message was sent

      You can retrieve up to one weekâs worth of messages.

    - Message processing status, such as `Received`
    - Acknowledgment status, such as `Overdue`
    - Partner message type, such as `X12 - 850`
    - Host message type, such as `JSON host-purchase-order`
    - Transmission ID
    - Whether transmissions are replays

      Partner Manager returns a list of transmissions that meet your search criteria along with
      related information, such as the date the transmission was received, number of messages in the
      transmission, message type, control number, and acknowledgment status.

5.  Do either of the following:
    - Click a transmission to review additional information about the transmission and to view its
      message flow.
    - Click **messages** to see information about the messages in the transaction, as described in
      [Activity Message Tracking](activity-message-tracking).

## View Details for an Inbound Transmission

The additional details for an inbound transmission include the following information:

- Transmission status (a green checkmark for success or a red exclamation point for failure)
- Transmission ID
- Control number associated with the transmitted file, if available
- Information about the message source, including the date and time that the message was received,
  control number, name and protocol of the receiving (Receive from Partners) endpoint, and message
  type, such as `mythical-po-ack`
- Number of received messages for each message type in the transmitted file
- Custom message attributes, if any.

If the transmission is a replay, the details also include a link to the original (replayed)
transmission.

If this transmission was replayed, the details also include the date and time of the most recent
replay.

Take any of the following actions to view and download additional information about the inbound
transmission:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>Steps</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View the original transmission if the message is a replay.</p></td><td><p>Click the link in the <strong>Original Transmission Id</strong> field.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View the messages in the transmission.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>messages</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the transmission payload received from the partner.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Received transmission</strong> field, click <strong>View Payload</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed, and click <strong>Apply</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the MDN message sent to the partner in response to an inbound AS2 transmission, if an MDN message exists.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Sent MDN</strong> field, click <strong>View MDN</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed, and click <strong>Apply</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the EDIFACT CONTRL or X12 997 acknowledgment message sent to the partner, if one exists.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Processed message through message flows</strong> field, click <strong>View CONTRL</strong> or <strong>View 997</strong> for each message type.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed, and click <strong>Apply</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View the message flow diagram for the transmission.</p></td><td><p>Click the link in the <strong>Processed message through message flows</strong> field to view the message flow diagram.<br><br>From the message flow diagram, you can expand the message flow sections to view additional information, including information about the receiving (Receive from Partners) endpoint, source message, DataWeave translation map, and target message type and endpoint.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## View Details for an Outbound Transmission

The details for an outbound transmission include the following information:

- Transmission status (a green checkmark for success or a red exclamation point for failure)
- Message ID
- Transmission ID
- Date and time when the file was sent
- Control number associated with the transmitted file, if one exists
- Message type, such as `mythical-po-ack-json: ORD-9892330`
- Name of the sending (Send to Partners) endpoint
- Name of the transmitted file
- Number of messages in the transmission

If this transmission is a replay, the details also include the transmission ID of the original
(replayed) transmission.

If this transmission was replayed, the details also include the date and time of the most recent
replay.

Take any of the following actions to view and download additional information about the outbound
transmission:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>Steps</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View the original transmission if the message is a replay.</p></td><td><p>Click the link in the <strong>Original Transmission Id field</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View information about the sending (Send to Partner) endpoint.</p></td><td><p>In the <strong>Summary</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>Endpoint</strong> field.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the transmission payload.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>Summary</strong> section, click the link in the <strong>File</strong> field.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the payload received from the backend.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Source</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>View Payload</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View information about the source (Source at Host) endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Source</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>Click the link in the <strong>Received payload</strong> section.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the DataWeave map that transformed the transmission.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Map</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>Click the link in this section.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View information about the message type, including its name, type, identifiers, and control numbers</p></td><td><div><div><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Message Type</strong> section.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the payload sent to the partner.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Sent to</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Sent transmission</strong> field, click <strong>View payload</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View and download the MDN received from the partner in response to outbound AS2 transmissions.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Sent to</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Received MDN</strong> field, click <strong>View payload</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View the EDIFACT CONTRL or X12 997 acknowledgment message received from the partner, if one exists.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Sent to</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Sent transmission</strong> field, click <strong>View payload</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Specify a wrap character, if needed.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Download</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>View the original transmission if the current transmission is a replay.</p></td><td><div><div><ol><li><p>In the message flow diagram, expand the <strong>Last replayed</strong> section.</p></li><li><p>Click the link in this section.</p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Replaying Transmissions](replay-transmissions)
- [Inbound Message Flows](inbound-message-flows)
- [Outbound Message Flows](outbound-message-flows)
- [EDI Acknowledgment Reconciliation](edi-ack-reconciliation)
- [Troubleshooting Anypoint Partner Manager](troubleshooting)
---

# Apply Environment Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can apply changes to your environmentâs Partner Manager or Access Management settings, like Mule
runtime version or vCore and ActiveMQ configurations, to all your runtime applications at once.
Follow these steps to apply the changes:

1.  From the Message flows page, click **Manage deployments**.
2.  Select **Apply runtime settings**.
3.  Check the box to confirm that you understand the effects of these actions.
4.  Click **Confirm**.

Applying environment settings updates all the Mule applications in your runtime with the latest
available template versions.
---

# Configuring Receive Settings for AS2 Inbound Messages

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The AS2 encoding setting determines the character encoding type for all messages received from this
partner.

To configure the encoding type:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Partners**.
2.  Select the partner to which the setting applies.
3.  In the **Global endpoint settings** sectionâs **Inbound endpoint settings** column, click
    **AS2**.
4.  Select one of the following encoding types from the **Character encoding override** dropdown:
    - NONE
    - ASCII
    - ISO-8859-1
    - ISO-8859-2
    - ISO-8859-3
    - ISO-8859-4
    - ISO-8859-5
    - ISO-8859-6
    - ISO-8859-7
    - ISO-8859-8
    - ISO-8859-9
    - UTF-8

5.  Click **Save**.
---

# AS2 Signature and Encryption Algorithms

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

AS2 endpoints deployed from Partner Manager support the following signature and encryption
algorithms:

- Signature algorithms
  - `UNSIGNED (Default)`
  - `MD5`
  - `SHA1`
  - `SHA224`
  - `SHA256`
  - `SHA384`
  - `SHA512`

- Encryption algorithms
  - `UNENCRYPTED (Default)`
  - `AES128_CBC`
  - `AES128_CCM`
  - `AES128_GCM`
  - `AES192_CBC`
  - `AES192_CCM`
  - `AES192_GCM`
  - `AES256_CBC`
  - `AES256_CCM`
  - `AES256_GCM`
  - `AES256_WRAP`
  - `CAST5`
  - `DES_EDE3`
  - `RC2`

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
---

# Certificate and Keystore Storage

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Certificates and keystores in Anypoint Partner Manager are securely stored in the Anypoint Platform
secrets manager, which uses secure vault technology to store and control access to private keys,
passwords, certificates, and other secrets.

For endpoints that use certificates or keystores, you can either select an available certificate or
keystore that works with the endpoint type and protocol or upload a new one to Partner Manager.

## See Also

- [Managing Host Certificates](manage-host-certificates)
- [Managing AS2 Host Keystores](manage-as2-host-keystores)
- [Managing Partner and Third-Party Connection Certificates](manage-partner-certificates)
---

# Configuring CloudHub 1.0 Deployment Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To configure settings for deploying Anypoint Partner Manager to CloudHub 1.0, access the business
unit and environment for which you want to deploy Partner Manager and then follow these steps:

1.  From the access management **Configure a Partner Manager environment** window, enter the
    following general settings:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Deployment Target Type</p></td><td><p>Target type for your Partner Manager deployment. Select <strong>CloudHub 1.0</strong>.</p></td><td><p>CloudHub 1.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Runtime Version</p></td><td><p>Mule runtime engine version to run Partner Manager applications.</p></td><td><div><div><p>4.6.0</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">If you select Mule runtime 4.9.0 LTS using Java 17, test your component versions and SFTP connections that rely on insecure ciphers to make sure they work as expected with Java 17.</div></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min Mule Version override</p></td><td><p>Minimum Mule runtime version required to deploy the runtime applications.</p><p>Configuring the Min Mule version enables you to apply a <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/feature-flagging#configure-feature-flags-for-a-mule-application">feature flagging</a> mechanism for Partner Manager-deployed Mule runtime applications.</p><p>If you process cXML messages with &lt;DOCTYPE&gt; tags, you must set the <code>minMuleVersion</code> to 4.5.0 or higher. If youâre migrating from an earlier version, you must perform end-to-end regression testing on your existing workflows before changing the <code>minMuleVersion</code>.</p></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">If you select <code>None</code>, Partner Manager defaults to using <code>4.3.0</code> as the Min Mule version.</div></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payload compression</p></td><td><p>Select not to compress the payload, always compress the payload, or adaptively compress the payload only when Anypoint MQ fails because the payload size exceeds a certain threshold.</p></td><td><p>None (not compressed)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CloudHub Region</p></td><td><p>Region in CloudHub to which you want to deploy Partner Manager runtime applications. You canât change the region if active message flows are deployed to the existing region. To change the region in this case, you must undeploy the message flows. You can redeploy the message flows after you change the region.</p></td><td><p>Retrieved from the business groupâs Runtime Manager setting</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anypoint MQ Region</p></td><td><p>This value is automatically assigned based on the specified CloudHub region and you canât change it.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Automatically restart app when not responding</p></td><td><p>Directs CloudHub to automatically restart your application when the monitoring system discovers an issue.</p></td><td><p>Selected</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Einstein enabled</p></td><td><p>Enables Einstein features in Partner Manager, such as the <a href="activity-message-tracking#message-summaries">message summarization</a> feature for source and target payloads.</p></td><td><p>Not selected</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Select **Applications** and enter the following application settings:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Each B2B endpoint</p></td><td><p>Reserved CPU space, CPU space limit, and memory capacity for each Partner Manager endpoint. Partner Manager deploys a generic Mule app to the runtime for each endpoint protocol and direction (receive or send) combination.</p></td><td><div><div><p>0.1 vCores</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>B2B API Replication Service</p></td><td><p>Reserved CPU space, CPU space limit, and memory capacity for the B2B API Replication Service.</p></td><td><p>0.2 vCores</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>B2B Document Processing Service</p></td><td><p>Reserved CPU space, CPU space limit, and memory capacity for the B2B Document Processing Service.</p></td><td><p>0.2 vCores</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deploy HTTP/AS2 apps to private port</p></td><td><p>Directs CloudHub to automatically deploy newly created AS2, HTTP, and HTTPS endpoints to a private port.</p></td><td><p>Selected</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    For help with sizing, contact your MuleSoft Account Team.

3.  Click **Save**.

## See Also

[Deploying Partner Manager Runtime Apps](deploy-partner-manager)
---

# Configuring CloudHub 2.0 Deployment Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To configure settings for deploying Anypoint Partner Manager to CloudHub 2.0, access the business
unit and environment for which you want to deploy Partner Manager and then follow these steps:

1.  From the Access Management **Configure a Partner Manager environment** window, enter these
    general settings:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Deployment Target Type</p></td><td><p>Target type for your Partner Manager deployment. Select <strong>CloudHub 2.0</strong>.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Runtime Version</p></td><td><p>Mule runtime engine version to run Partner Manager applications.</p></td><td><div><div><p>4.6.0 LTS using Java 8</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">If you select Mule runtime 4.9.0 LTS using Java 17, test your component versions and SFTP connections that rely on insecure ciphers to make sure they work as expected with Java 17.</div></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min Mule Version override</p></td><td><p>Minimum Mule runtime version required to deploy the runtime applications.</p><p>Configuring the Min Mule version enables you to apply a <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/feature-flagging#configure-feature-flags-for-a-mule-application">feature flagging</a> mechanism for Partner Manager-deployed Mule runtime applications.</p><p>If you process cXML messages with &lt;DOCTYPE&gt; tags, you must set the <code>minMuleVersion</code> to 4.5.0 or higher. If youâre migrating from an earlier version, you must perform end-to-end regression testing on your existing workflows before changing the <code>minMuleVersion</code>.</p></td><td><div><div><p>None (Uses 4.3.0)</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">If you select <code>None</code>, Partner Manager defaults to using <code>4.3.0</code> as the Min Mule version.</div></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payload compression</p></td><td><p>Select not to compress the payload, always compress the payload, or adaptively compress the payload only when Anypoint MQ fails because the payload size exceeds a certain threshold.</p></td><td><p>None (not compressed)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Deployment Target</p></td><td><p>Shared or private space to which you want to deploy Partner Manager runtime applications. You can change the deployment target after all active message flows are no longer deployed to an existing target. To change the region in these instances, undeploy the message flows, change the region, and then redeploy the message flows.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Einstein enabled</p></td><td><p>Enables Einstein features in Partner Manager, such as the <a href="activity-message-tracking#message-summaries">message summarization</a> feature for source and target payloads.</p></td><td><p>Not selected</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Select **Applications** and enter these application settings:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Each B2B endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>Reserved CPU space, CPU space limit, and memory capacity for each Partner Manager endpoint application. Partner Manager deploys a generic Mule app to the runtime for each endpoint protocol and direction (receive or send) combination.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">Partner manager deploys each AS2 and HTTPS inbound endpoint configuration as a separate Mule application. You can reuse the same endpoint configuration across multiple partners and message types.</div></div></div></td><td><p>0.1 vCores</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>B2B API Replication Service</p></td><td><p>Reserved CPU space, CPU space limit, and memory capacity for the B2B API Replication Service.</p></td><td><p>0.2 vCores</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>B2B Document Processing Service</p></td><td><p>Reserved CPU space, CPU space limit, and memory capacity for the B2B Document Processing Service.</p></td><td><p>0.2 vCores</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    For help with sizing, contact your MuleSoft Account Team.

3.  Click **Save**.

## See Also

[Deploying Partner Manager Runtime Apps](deploy-partner-manager)
---

# Configuring Global EDIFACT Receive Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

When EDIFACT messages received from your partners do not contain a UNA segment, Partner Manager uses
the delimiters specified in the **Global EDIFACT settings: Receive from partners** window to parse
the received EDIFACT payloads. You define these settings in the host organizationâs profile:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Data element separator</p></td><td><p>Data element separator character</p></td><td><p>+</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Component separator</p></td><td><p>Component element separator character</p></td><td><p>:</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Repetition separator</p></td><td><p>Repetition separator character</p></td><td><p>*</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Segment terminator</p></td><td><p>Segment terminator character</p></td><td><p>'</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Release character</p></td><td><p>Release character</p></td><td><p>?</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

[Configuring and Managing a Host Profile](configure-host)
---

# Configuring and Managing a Host Profile

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Your Anypoint Platform organization or your business group is referred to as the _host_ in Anypoint
Partner Manager. In Anypoint Platform, your host name is the same as your Anypoint Platform business
group name.

Use the **<host>** page in Partner Manager to:

- Add details about your organization, such as its name, website, logo, corporate address, and
  contacts.
- [Add the EDI and AS2 identifiers](partner-manager-identifiers) used by your organization.
- [Manage the host certificates](manage-host-certificates) associated with your organization.
- [Manage the AS2 host keystores](manage-as2-host-keystores) associated with your organization, if
  you plan to use AS2 endpoints.
- [Create and manage source and target endpoints](create-endpoint).
- [Create and manage source and target message types](partner-manager-create-message-type).
- [Configure a payload storage API](setup-payload-storage-api) to provide Partner Manager with a
  standardized way to connect to your storage.
- [Configure global settings for EDIFACT receive messages](configure-global-edifact-receive-settings).

## See Also

- [Configuring and Managing Partners](configure-partner)
---

# Configuring and Managing Partners

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use the **Partners** page to create and manage partners. From this page, you can:

- [Create a new partner](create-partner).
- [Add the EDI and AS2 identifiers](partner-manager-identifiers) used by your partner.
- [Manage the digital certificates](manage-partner-certificates) associated with your partner.
- [Create](create-endpoint) and manage source and target endpoints owned by the partner.
- Manage EDI validation settings for [X12 receive settings](x12-receive-read-settings),
  [EDIFACT receive settings](edifact-receive-read-settings), [X12 send settings](x12-send-settings),
  and [EDIFACT send settings](edifact-send-settings).
- [Modify partner settings](modify-partner-settings).
- [Delete a partner](partner-manager-delete-partner).

## See Also

- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
- [Troubleshooting Anypoint Partner Manager](troubleshooting)
---

# Configuring and Managing Third-Party Connections

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If your partners use third-party service providers such as VANs or 3PLs (third-party logistics
providers) who manage the B2B connectivity with your organization on behalf of your partners, you
can create a third-party connection to manage configurations such as the endpoints, identifiers, and
certificates of the third-party provider.

Use the **Partners** page to create and manage third-party connections. From this page, you can:

- [Create a new third-party connection](create-third-party).
- [Add the EDI and AS2 identifiers](partner-manager-identifiers) used by the third-party connection.
- [Manage the digital certificates](manage-partner-certificates) associated with the third-party
  connection.
- [Create](create-endpoint) and manage source and target endpoints owned by the third-party
  connection.
- [Modify third-party connection settings](modify-third-party-settings).
- [Delete a third-party connection](delete-third-party).

After you create a third-party connection, you can select the endpoints and identifiers configured
for that connection in message flows created for your partners.

## See Also

- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
- [Creating Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow)
- [Troubleshooting Anypoint Partner Manager](troubleshooting)
---

# Creating Endpoints

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can create new endpoints from the **Partners**, **<host>**, or third-party page, or by using the
Message Flow wizard.

## Create Endpoints from the Partners Page

Use the **Partners** page to create new send and receive endpoints owned by partners or third-party
connections:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Partners**.
2.  Select the partner or third-party connection for which to create the endpoint.
3.  In the **Endpoints** section, click **New**.
4.  In the **New Endpoint** window **Usage** field, select **Receive from partners** or **Send to
    partners**.
5.  In the **Protocol** field, select the protocol to use for the endpoint.
6.  [Configure the endpoint](#configure-endpoint) according to the endpoint type and protocol.
7.  Click **Save**.

Ownership of endpoints is automatically assigned based on the partner or third-party connection from
which you are creating the endpoint.

Partner Manager environments support up to 2,000 endpoints. To maximize your environmentâs capacity,
reuse endpoint configurations across partners and message flows.

## Create Endpoints from the Host Page

Use the **<host>** page to create new send and receive endpoints owned by the host:

1.  In the sidebar, select the host.
2.  In the **Endpoints** section, click **New**.
3.  In the **New Endpoint** window **Usage** field, select the endpoint type.
4.  In the **Protocol** field, select the protocol to use for the endpoint.
5.  [Configure the endpoint](#configure-endpoint) according to the endpoint type and protocol.
6.  Click **Save**.

## Create Endpoints Using the Message Flow Wizard

You can use the Message Flow Wizard to create send and receive endpoints owned by hosts, partners,
or third-party connections.

For information about using the Message Flow wizard to create new endpoints, see
[Create Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow) and
[Create Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow).

## Protocol-Specific Endpoint Settings

The values for the endpointâs settings vary according to the endpoint type and protocol:

- [AS2 Receive Endpoint Settings](endpoint-as2-receive)
- [AS2 Send Endpoint Settings](endpoint-as2-send)
- [FTP Receive Endpoint Settings](endpoint-ftp-receive)
- [FTP Send Endpoint Settings](endpoint-ftp-send)
- [HTTP and HTTPS Receive Endpoint Settings](endpoint-https-receive)
- [HTTP and HTTPS Send Endpoint Settings](endpoint-https-send)
- [SFTP Receive Endpoint Settings](endpoint-sftp-receive-target)
- [SFTP Send Endpoint Settings](endpoint-sftp-send)

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Deploying Receive Endpoints](deploying-receive-endpoints)
- [Modifying Endpoints](modify-endpoints)
- [Deleting Endpoints](delete-endpoints)
---

# Creating Inbound Message Flows

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Create inbound message flows to receive messages from partners, including messages that go through
third-party connections. When you create an inbound message flow, you can validate and transform the
inbound messages and then send them to your backend applications.

Anypoint Partner Manager routes inbound messages based on matching identifiers configured for the
partner.

Before you create your first message flow, configure your
[payload storage API](setup-payload-storage-api).

To create an inbound message flow:

1.  From the Partner Manager sidebar, select **Message Flows**.
2.  Click **New message flow > Receive from partner**.
3.  Either select an existing partner from the list or select **New** to create a new partner, as
    described in [Create a Partner](create-partner).
4.  Configure the receiving endpoint.
5.  Configure the source message type.
6.  Select the translation map.
7.  Configure the target message type.
8.  Configure the target endpoint.
9.  Verify that the message flow is complete.

## Configure the Receiving Endpoint

The receiving endpoint (Receive from Partners endpoint) in an inbound message flow receives the
messages that your partners send, either directly or via third-party connections. To configure a
receiving endpoint, you can either select an existing endpoint owned by the host, partner, or
third-party connection, or you can create a new endpoint.

Partner Manager supports the AS2, FTP, HTTP, HTTPS, and SFTP formats for the receiving endpoint in
an inbound message flow.

To configure the receiving endpoint:

1.  In the message flow, expand the **Receiving endpoint** section and click **Select**.
2.  In the **Select an endpoint: Receive from <partner>** window, do either of the following:
    - Select an existing Receive from Partners endpoint and click **Select**.
    - Click **New** to create a new Receive from Partners endpoint, as described in
      [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint).

      If you select an endpoint owned by a third-party connection, the messages go through that
      connection.

      After you select an endpoint, it is added to the message flow.

If the protocol of the receiving endpoint is AS2, perform these additional steps:

1.  Select the AS2 sender from the dropdown menu and then select either of the following:
    - The partnerâs name, to enable your organization to receive the AS2 messages directly from the
      partner for whom the message flow is created
    - The name of a third-party connection, to enable your organization to receive the partnerâs AS2
      messages via a third-party connection, such as a VAN or 3PL

      The AS2 identifier and certificate of the selected third-party connection are used for
      validating the AS2 messages.

2.  If there is no valid certificate in the associated profile, import one from the partner or from
    the third-party connection sending the AS2 message.

## Configure the Source Message Type

The source message type (Receive from Partners message type) in an inbound message flow defines the
structure of the messages that Partner Manager receives from a partner or third-party connection.
Partner Manager supports the CSV, EDIFACT, JSON, X12, and XML formats for source message types in an
inbound message flow.

To configure the source message type:

1.  In the message flow, expand the **Source message** section and click **Select**.
2.  In the **Select message type** window, do either of the following:
    - Select an existing Receive from Partners message type and click **Select**.
    - Click **New** to create a new Receive from Partners message type, as described in
      [Creating Message Types](partner-manager-create-message-type).

    The message type and its validation settings are added to the message flow.

If the source message type is X12 or EDIFACT and your partner expects your organization to return
functional acknowledgments, perform these additional steps:

1.  In the **Acknowledgement** section, select the checkbox next to **Send 997 functional
    acknowledgement (X12)** or **Send CONTRL functional acknowledgement (EDIFACT)**.
2.  Click **Select**.
3.  In the **Select an endpoint: Send acknowledgement to <partner>** window, do either of the
    following:
    - Select the endpoint to receive the acknowledgment messages from your partner and click
      **Select**.
    - Click **New** to create a new endpoint, as described in [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint).

    The acknowledgment is added to the message flow.

If the source message type is:

- X12, select the partner and host organizationâs ISA (Interchange) identifiers from the dropdown
  menu.
- EDIFACT, select the partner and host organizationâs UNA (Interchange) identifiers from the
  dropdown menu.
- CSV, JSON, or XML, select the partner and host organizationâs reference identifiers from the
  dropdown menu.

To route inbound messages from partners matching any of the partner or host identifiers, select
**All identifiers** from the dropdown menu. For more information about the order of precedence in
inbound message routing, see [Inbound Message Routing](inbound-message-routing).

## Select the Translation Map

A translation map in an inbound message flow transforms your inbound message format to your
organizationâs enterprise application format. Maps are created using DataWeave and are imported to
the [Maps](maps) page. For more information about creating maps, see
[Translation Maps](partner-manager-maps).

To select the map:

1.  In the message flow, expand the **Map** section.
2.  Click **Select** and select the map from the table.

The map is added to the message flow. Only the mapping configurations that match your source and
target message types are shown. Choosing a map configuration with a different source and target
message type updates the corresponding message type in your message flow.

In the map for an inbound message flow, you can access source message attributes, which makes them
available in the message payload. The attributes that you can access depend on the protocol of the
source endpoint. For more information, see
[Accessing Source Message Attributes](access-source-message-attributes).

## Configure the Target Message Type

The target message type (Target at Host message type) in an inbound message flow defines the
structure of the messages that Partner Manager sends to the backend applications. Partner Manager
supports the CSV, JSON, and XML formats for the target message type in an inbound message flow.

To configure the target message type:

1.  In the message flow, expand the **Target at <host>** section.
2.  In the **Message Type** section, click **Select**.
3.  In the **Select message type** window, do either of the following:
    - Select an existing Target at Host message type and click **Select**.
    - Click **New** to create a new Target at Host message type.

4.  Click **Save**.

## Configure the Target Endpoint

The target endpoint (Target at Host Endpoint) in an inbound message flow receives the transformed
messages on the backend applications. Partner Manager supports the FTP, HTTP, HTTPS, and SFTP
formats for the target endpoint in an inbound message flow.

To configure the target endpoint:

1.  In the message flow, expand the **Target at <host>** section.
2.  In the **Target at <host>** section, expand the **Endpoint** section and click **Select**.
3.  In the **Select an endpoint: Target to <host>** window, do either of the following:
    - Select an existing Target at Host endpoint and click **Select**.
    - Click **New** to create a new Target at Host endpoint, as described in
      [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint).

    After you select an endpoint, it is added to the message flow.

## Verify That the Message Flow Is Complete

Partner Manager dynamically validates the message flow configuration elements for completeness and
displays a green checkmark if all of the message flow building blocks are complete. After you verify
the message flow configuration, you can deploy and test the message flow.

## See Also

- [Inbound Message Flows](inbound-message-flows)
- [Deploying and Testing Message Flows](deploy-message-flows)
- [Modifying Message Flow Settings](manage-message-flows)
- [Accessing Source Message Attributes](access-source-message-attributes)
- [Inbound Message Routing](inbound-message-routing)
---

# Creating Outbound Message Flows

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Create outbound message flows to receive messages from your backend applications, validate and
transform these messages to the format your partners expect, and then send them to your partners
either directly, or through third-party connections.

Before you create your first message flow, configure your
[payload storage API](setup-payload-storage-api).

To create an outbound message flow:

1.  From the Anypoint Partner Manager sidebar, select **Message Flows**.
2.  Click **New message flow > Send to partner**.
3.  Either select an existing partner or select **New** to create a new partner, as described in
    [Create a Partner](create-partner).
4.  Configure the receiving endpoint.
5.  Configure the source message type.
6.  Configure reference IDs for the partner and host.
7.  Select the translation map.
8.  Configure the target message type.
9.  Configure the target endpoint.
10. Verify that the message flow is complete.

## Configure the Receiving Endpoint

The receiving endpoint (Source at Host endpoint) in an outbound message flow receives application
messages from the backend applications. Partner Manager supports the FTP, HTTP, HTTPS, and SFTP
formats for the receiving endpoint.

To configure the receiving endpoint:

1.  In the message flow, expand the **Source at <host-name>** section and click **Select**.
2.  In the **Select an endpoint: Source at <host-name>** window, do either of the following:
    - Select an existing Source at Host endpoint and click **Select**.
    - Click **New** to create a new Source at Host endpoint, as described in
      [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint).

## Configure the Source Message Type

The source message type (Source at Host message type) in an outbound message flow defines the
structure of the messages that Partner Manager receives from the backend applications. Partner
Manager translates these messages to a configured Send to Partners message type, and then sends them
to your partners, either directly or through a third-party connection.

Partner Manager supports the CSV, JSON, and XML source message types in outbound message flows.

To create the source message type:

1.  In the message flow, expand the **Source message type** section and click **Select**.
2.  In the **Source message type** window, do either of the following:
    - Select an existing Source at Host message type and click **Select**.
    - Click **New** to create a new Source at Host message type, as described in
      [Creating Message Types](partner-manager-create-message-type).

## Configure Reference IDs for the Partner and Host

Partner Manager uses the partner reference ID and host reference ID to locate the appropriate
message flow for routing outbound messages. For information about how this routing process works,
see [Sender and Receiver Identifiers](partner-manager-identifiers) and
[Outbound Message Routing](outbound-message-routing).

To configure a partner reference ID, either select **All available identifiers** or select a
specific partner identifier.

To configure a host reference ID, either select **Ignore** or select your organizationâs internal
reference ID (such as the business unit name) as it appears in the data element of the source JSON
or XML message from your backend applications.

## Select the Translation Map

A translation map in an outbound message flow transforms outbound messages from your organizationâs
enterprise application format to the format your partners expect. Maps are created using DataWeave
and are imported to the [Maps](maps) page. For more information about creating maps, see
[Translation Maps](partner-manager-maps).

To select the map:

1.  In the message flow, expand the **Map** section.
2.  Click **Select** and select the map from the table.

The map is added to the message flow. Only the mapping configurations that match your source and
target message types are shown. Choosing a map configuration with a different source and target
message type updates the corresponding message type in your message flow.

In the map for an outbound message flow, you can access source message attributes, which makes them
available in the message payload. The attributes that you can access depend on the protocol of the
source endpoint. For more information, see
[Accessing Source Message Attributes](access-source-message-attributes).

## Configure the Target Message Type

The target message type (Send to Partners message type) in an outbound message flow defines the
structure of the messages that Partner Manager sends to the partner, either directly or via a
third-party connection. Partner Manager supports the CSV, EDIFACT, JSON, X12, and XML formats for
target message types.

For the EDIFACT and X12 message types, configuring the target message type also involves the
following:

- Configuring acknowledgments (optional)
- Creating custom EDIFACT settings (optional)
- Selecting EDIFACT identifiers to use for routing the outbound messages

To configure the target message type:

1.  Expand the **Message type** section and click **Select**.
2.  In the **Select message type** window, do either of the following:
    - Select an existing Send to Partners message type and click **Select**.
    - Click **New** to create a new Send to Partners message type, as described in
      [Creating Message Types](partner-manager-create-message-type).

3.  Click **Save**.

    The message type and its validation settings are added to the message flow.

4.  Optionally configure acknowledgment settings for the outbound message.
5.  Optionally apply custom validation settings delimiters and character encoding to the outbound
    message flow.
6.  In the **<protocol> Identifiers** section, select the sender and receiver identifiers to use on
    either the X12 ISA and GS segments or the EDIFACT UNB segments.

    These identifiers are configured in the host and partner profiles.

### Configure Acknowledgment Settings

To configure acknowledgment settings for the outbound messages that use the message flow:

1.  In the **Acknowledgement** section, select **Expect functional acknowledgement** (X12) or
    **Expect CONTRL functional acknowledgement** (EDIFACT).
2.  Do either of the following:
    - Select an existing endpoint at which to receive your partnerâs acknowledgment transaction.
    - Click **New** to create a new endpoint, as described in [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint).

3.  In the **Mark overdue after** field, accept the default value of **24 Hours** or configure when
    to mark the outbound transaction as overdue for an acknowledgment, according to the SLAs you
    have with your partner.

### Optionally Apply Custom Validation Settings to EDIFACT and X12 Endpoints

By default, Partner Manager uses the EDIFACT or X12 send settings configured for the partner or
third-party connection for message validation, delimiters, and character encoding.

To apply custom validation settings and delimiters to the outbound message flow, follow these steps:

1.  In the **EDIFACT Settings** section, select either **Custom X12 settings** or **Custom EDIFACT
    settings** and provide custom configuration values.
2.  In the **<protocol> Identifiers** section, select the sender and receiver identifiers to use on
    either the X12 ISA and GS segments or the EDIFACT UNB segments.

    These identifiers are configured in the host and partner profiles.

## Configure the Target Endpoint

The Target endpoint (Send to Partners endpoint) is the target endpoint to which transformed messages
are sent to partners, either directly or via third-party connections. Use a third-party-owned Send
to Partners endpoint for partners that use a third-party connection.

Partner Manager supports the EDIFACT and X12 protocols for Send to Partners endpoints.

To configure the target endpoint:

1.  In the message flow, expand the **Send to <partner>** section and click **Select**.
2.  Do either of the following:
    - Select an existing Send to Partners endpoint and click **Select**.

      You can select any previously configured Send to Partners endpoints owned by the host, the
      partner for whom the message flow is configured, or a third-party connection.

    - Click **New** to create a new Send to Partners endpoint, as described in
      [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint).

If you selected AS2 as the protocol and there is no certificate associated with the partner or
third-party profile, import the certificate of the partner or third-party connection that owns the
endpoint.

## Verify That the Message Flow Is Complete

Partner Manager dynamically validates the message flow configuration elements as you complete them
and displays a green checkmark next to each of the validated elements. After you verify the
configurations, you are ready to deploy the message flow.

## See Also

- [Outbound Message Flows](outbound-message-flows)
- [Outbound Message Routing](outbound-message-routing)
- [Deploying and Testing Message Flows](deploy-message-flows)
- [Modifying Message Flow Settings](manage-message-flows)
---

# Creating Partners

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Create a new partner by using the **Partners** page to specify partner-specific information such as
name, identifiers, endpoints, and validation rules.

Before you create your first partner,
[make sure that you have configured your payload storage API](setup-payload-storage-api) and that
you have the following information:

- The partnerâs name (usually the name of the company)
- One or more partner identifiers (such as the AS2 ID, X12-ISA, X12 GS, and so on)

To create a partner:

1.  In Partner Manager, select **Partners** in the sidebar.
2.  Click **Create partner > Partner**.
3.  Enter the following information:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><p>Text string that uniquely identifies your partner</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Identifier type</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Qualifier</strong></p></td><td><p>Value that appears if you selected <strong>X12-ISA</strong> or <strong>EDIFACT-UNB</strong> as the type</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Identifier Value</strong></p></td><td><p>Value that uniquely identifies your partner in the EDI stream</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Click **Create partner**.

Partner Manager environments support a maximum of 1,000 partners each. To onboard more partners,
create an additional environment.

## Configure Additional Partner Settings

Add the following settings for the new partner, either now or later on:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Settings</th><th>Action</th><th>Additional Information</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Information about the partner, such as the partnerâs address, website, and contact details.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>Edit</strong> in the <strong>About</strong> section.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional identifiers to associate with the partner.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Identifiers</strong> section.</p></td><td><p><a href="partner-manager-identifiers">Sender and Receiver Identifiers</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Partnerâs digital certificates, such as X.509 certificates.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Certificates</strong> section.</p></td><td><p><a href="manage-partner-certificates">Managing Partner and Third-Party Connection Certificates</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Partner-specific endpoints to use for sending and receiving messages to and from your partner, including acknowledgment endpoints. If you plan to use host endpoints only, you donât need to create partner-specific endpoints.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Endpoints</strong> section.</p></td><td><p><a href="create-endpoint">Creating Endpoints</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Acknowledgments, validations, and control numbers for each message format that the partner uses. For EDIFACT and X12, you can also configure character sets and separators (send formats only).</p></td><td><p>Click the links in the <strong>Validation and acknowledgement</strong> settings.</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="edifact-receive-read-settings">EDIFACT Receive Settings</a></p></li><li><p><a href="edifact-send-settings">EDIFACT Send Settings</a></p></li><li><p><a href="x12-receive-read-settings">X12 Receive Settings</a></p></li><li><p><a href="x12-send-settings">X12 Send Settings</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character encoding override option for received AS2 messages.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>AS2</strong> in <strong>Global endpoint settings</strong>.</p></td><td><p><a href="as2-encoding">AS2 Receive Settings</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Next Step

After you create a partner and configure partner-level endpoints (if needed), you can set up message
flows.

## See Also

- [Configuring and Managing Message Flows](message-flows)
- [Creating Partners](create-partner)
- [Modifying Partner Settings](modify-partner-settings)
- [Deleting Partners](partner-manager-delete-partner)
---

# Creating Reference Maps for Message Types

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

When you create a new Source at Host or Receive from Partners message type with a CSV, JSON, or XML
format, you must associate the message type with a DataWeave reference map. This is optional for
Receive from Partners message types with an EDFIACT or X12 format. Reference maps can include custom
message attributes that are extracted from the message payloads at runtime. The extracted attributes
are visible in the payload and are available for searching and tracking.

If the definition for the message type includes an uploaded schema, the input of the reference map
is the payload that follows the schema.

The output of the reference map contains the following fields:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Field</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Required or Optional</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>partnerReferenceId</p></td><td><p>Identifier that uniquely identifies the partner</p></td><td><div><div><p>Required for:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>All Source at Host message types</p></li><li><p>Receive from Partners message types with a CSV. JSON, or XML format</p></li></ul></div><div><p>You can hardcode this value as a static value, depending on your data processing and routing needs.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>hostReferenceId</p></td><td><p>Identifier that uniquely identifies the host</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Required for:</p><div><ul><li><p>Receive from Partners message types with a CSV, JSON, or XML format</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Optional for:</p><div><ul><li><p>Receive from Partners message types formatted with an EDIFACT or X12 format</p></li><li><p>All Source at Host message types</p></li></ul></div></li></ul></div><div><p>You can hardcode this value as a static value, depending on your data processing and routing needs.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>businessDocumentKey.</p></td><td><p>Key reference value, such as <code>Order Number</code> or <code>Invoice number</code></p></td><td><p>Optional</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>customAttributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of custom message attributes to extract from the payload. You can specify up to 100 custom message attributes in a given environment and can make up to 25 of them searchable.<br><br>When specifying a custom message attribute, configure the following fields in the reference map:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>alias</p><div><p>Alias that uniquely identifies a pre-configured custom message attribute configuration. The aliases in the reference map must match the message typeâs attribute definitions. If the map contains aliases that donât exist, creating or updating the message type fails.</p></div></li><li><p>values</p><div><p>An array of values, such as order numbers or invoice numbers.<br><br>Use an array of strings, even if there is only one attribute to extract. Using an array enables support for transactions with one-to-many relationships, such as transactions in which one shipment notice includes multiple purchase order numbers.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The output of a reference map must conform to this validation schema:

%dw 2.0 output application/json

---

{ "definitions": {}, "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#", "title": "Root", "type":
"object",

    "properties": {
    	"partnerReferenceId": {
    		"type": "string",
    		"default": "",
    		"pattern": "^.\*$"
    	},
    	"hostReferenceId": {
    		"type": \["string", "null"\],
    		"default": "",
    		"pattern": "^.\*$"
    	},
    	"businessDocumentKey": {
    		"type": \["string", "null"\],
    		"default": "",
    		"pattern": "^.\*$"
    	},
    	"customAttributes": {
    		"type": "array",
    		"default": \[\],
    		"items":{
    			"type": "object",
    			"required": \[
    				"alias",
    				"values"
    			\],
    			"properties": {
    				"alias": {
    					"type": "string",
    					"default": "",
    					"pattern": "^.\*$"
    				},
    				"values": {
    					"type": "array",
    					"default": \[\],
    					"items":{
    						"type": "string",
    						"default": "",
    						"pattern": "^.\*$"
    					}
    				}
    			}
    		}

    	}
    }

}

If the output of a reference map does not conform to the validation schema, the custom message
attributes wonât appear in the Partner Manager GUI. To ensure that the output of a reference map
conforms to the validation schema, you can add an Anypoint Connector for X12 EDI (X12 Connector)
**Validate schema** operation to your MuleSoft app, as shown in these Exchange examples:

- [B2B/EDI: Order to Cash - Mapping Examples](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.muleesb.modules/b2b-order-to-cash-mapping/)
- [B2B/EDI: Procure to Pay Mapping Examples](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.muleesb.modules/b2b-procure-to-pay-mappings/)

When you create a reference map, you can access other source message attributes in those maps. For
information, see [Accessing Source Message Attributes](access-source-message-attributes).

## Example Map for a Receive from Partners Message Type

The following map transforms a partnerâs inbound X12 payload. The map contains a business document
key and two custom message attributes:

- The value of the business document key is
  `payload.TransactionSets.v004010."855".Heading."020_BAK".BAK03[0]`.
- The value of the `purchaseOrderNumber` custom message attribute is
  `payload.TransactionSets.v004010."855".Heading."020_BAK".BAK03[0]`.
- The value of the `vendorSalesOrderNumber` custom message attribute is
  `payload.TransactionSets.v004010."855".Heading."020_BAK".BAK08[0]`.

The input to the map looks like this:

%dw 2.0 output application/json { businessDocumentKey:
payload.TransactionSets.v004010."855".Heading."020_BAK".BAK03\[0\] default "", customAttributes: \[
{ alias: "purchaseOrderNumber", values:
\[payload.TransactionSets.v004010."855".Heading."020_BAK".BAK03\[0\]\] }, { alias:
"vendorSalesOrderNumber", values:
\[payload.TransactionSets.v004010."855".Heading."020_BAK".BAK08\[0\]\] } \] }

The generated output of the map looks like this:

{ "businessDocumentKey": "ORDER-70010", "customAttributes": \[ { "alias": "purchaseOrderNumber",
"values": \[ "ORDER-70010" \] }, { "alias": "vendorSalesOrderNumber", "values": \[ "SO-5100803142"
\] } \] }

## Example Map for a Source at Host Message Type

The following map transforms the outbound JSON or XML payload from the backend. The map contains the
partner reference ID, host reference Id, business document key, and three custom attributes:

- The value of the partner reference ID is `payload.Header.CustomerName`.
- The value of the host reference ID is `payload.Invoice.Header.LineOfBusiness`.
- The value of the business document key is `payload.Invoice.Header.InvoiceNumber`.
- The value of the `invoiceNumber` custom attribute is `payload.Invoice.Header.InvoiceNumber`.
- The value of the `purchaseOrderNumber` custom message attribute is
  `payload.Invoice.Header.PurchaseOrderNumber`.
- The value of the `invoiceAmount` custom message attribute is
  `payload.Invoice.Header.TotalInvoiceAmt`.

The input to the map looks like this:

%dw 2.0 output application/json

---

{ partnerReferenceId: payload.Invoice.Header.CustomerName, hostReferenceId:
payload.Invoice.Header.LineOfBusiness default "MYTHICAL", businessDocumentKey:
payload.Invoice.Header.InvoiceNumber, customAttributes: \[ { alias: "invoiceNumber", values:
\[payload.Invoice.Header.InvoiceNumber default "UNKNOWN"\] }, { alias: "purchaseOrderNumber",
values: \[payload.Invoice.Header.PurchaseOrderNumber default "UNKNOWN"\] }, { alias:
"invoiceAmount", values: \[ "\\$ " ++ payload.Invoice.Header.TotalInvoiceAmt default "UNKNOWN"\] }
\] }

The generated output of the map looks like this:

{ "partnerReferenceId": "ALPHA-CAN", "hostReferenceId": "Mythical-MG", "businessDocumentKey":
"INV97750061", "customAttributes": \[ { "alias": "invoiceNumber", "values": \[ "INV97750061" \] }, {
"alias": "purchaseOrderNumber", "values": \[ "ORD3893988" \] }, { "alias": "invoiceAmount",
"values": \[ "$ 3756.24" \] } \] }

## Exchange Examples of DataWeave Maps

For examples of DataWeave maps on Exchange that include custom message attributes, see:

- [B2B/EDI: Order to Cash - Mapping Examples](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.muleesb.modules/b2b-order-to-cash-mapping/)
- [B2B/EDI: Procure to Pay Mapping Examples](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.muleesb.modules/b2b-procure-to-pay-mappings/)

## See Also

- [Creating a Message Type](partner-manager-create-message-type)
- [Modifying Message Type Settings](modify-message-type-settings)
- [Using Custom Message Attributes with Message Types](use-custom-attributes)
---

# Creating Third-Party Connections

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Create a new third-party connection by using the **Partners** page to specify the connectionâs name,
