identifiers, connection-specific endpoints (if needed), and other settings.

Before you create your first third-party connection, make sure that you have
[configured your payload storage API](setup-payload-storage-api) and that you obtain the following
information:

- The connection name (usually the name of the company)
- One or more AS2 identifiers for the connection

To create a third-party connection:

1.  In Partner Manager, select **Partners** in the sidebar.
2.  Click **Create partner > 3rd party connection**.
3.  Enter the following information:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><p>Text string that uniquely identifies the connection</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Identifier type</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Identifier Value</strong></p></td><td><p>Either a value that uniquely identifies the connection within the EDI stream or the AS2 identifier (if the connection uses AS2)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Click **Create 3rd party connection**.

## Configure Additional Connection Settings

Add the following settings for the new third-party connection, either now or later on:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Settings</th><th>To access this setting</th><th>Additional Information</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Information about the connection, such as the address, website, and contact details for the third-party company.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>Edit</strong> in the <strong>About</strong> section.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional identifiers to associate with the connection.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Identifiers</strong> section.</p></td><td><p><a href="partner-manager-identifiers">Sender and Receiver Identifiers</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>The connectionâs digital certificates, such as X.509 certificates.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Certificates</strong> section.</p></td><td><p><a href="manage-partner-certificates">Managing Partner and Third-Party Connection Certificates</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>The connection-specific endpoints to use for sending and receiving messages through the connection. If you plan to use host-owned endpoints only, you donât need to create connection-specific endpoints.</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Endpoints</strong> section.</p></td><td><p><a href="create-endpoint">Creating Endpoints</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Next Step

After you create a third-party connection and configure third-party-specific endpoints (if needed),
you can set up message flows that use the connection.

## See Also

- [Partner Manager Message Flows](message-flows)
- [Modifying Third-Party Connection Settings](modify-third-party-settings)
- [Deleting Third-Party Connections](delete-third-party)
---

# Deleting Endpoints

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can delete endpoints from the host or partner page if the endpoints are not used in message
flows:

1.  From Anypoint Partner Manager, do either of the following:
    - If the host owns the endpoint, select the **<host name>** from the sidebar.
    - If a partner or third-party connection owns the endpoint, select **Partners** from the sidebar
      and then select the owning partner or third-party connection.

2.  In the **Endpoints** section, locate the endpoint that you want to delete.

    To locate endpoints that donât have associated message flows, look for `-` in the **Message
    Flows** column.

3.  In the row containing the endpoint that you want to delete, hover over the endpoint name, select
    the trash can icon that appears, and click **Delete**.

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
- [Modifying Endpoints](modify-endpoints)
- [Creating Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow)
---

# Deleting Identifiers

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can delete identifiers such as X12 ISA, X12 GS, AS2, and DUNS.

Although you must always have at least one identifier per message flow, you can delete identifiers
that are no longer used in that message flow.

Before you delete an identifier, consider the following:

- You cannot delete an identifier that is used in a message flow.
- Deleting an existing host or partner identifier impacts the identification and routing of B2B
  messages to the associated partner.
- If you need to replace the identifier with another identifier, record the required values and
  qualifier type.

## Steps

1.  In the sidebar, select **Partners**.
2.  Select the partner containing the identifier to delete.
3.  In the **Identifiers** row, hover over the identifier to delete and click the trash can icon.

## See Also

- [Sender and Receiver Identifiers](partner-manager-identifiers)
---

# Deleting Message Flows

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can delete message flows that no longer serve their purpose, for example, if your relationship
with your partner changes. You can delete message flows from any environment.

Deleting a message flow automatically undeploys the flow.

To delete a message flow:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Message Flows**.
2.  Select the message flow to delete and click **Delete** in the upper right of the screen.

Transmissions already sent by a message flow that is subsequently deleted remain recorded in the
**Activity page**. Links to the stored payloads (such as the original B2B message), TA1
acknowledgments, the transformed payload, and so on, continue to work.

## See Also

- [Undeploying Message Flows](undeploy-message-flows)
---

# Deleting Message Types

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can delete message types from the **<host>** page if they are not used in message flows:

1.  In the sidebar, select **<host-name>**.
2.  In the **Message type** section, locate the message type you want to delete.

    To locate message types that donât have associated message flows, look for `-` in the **Message
    Flows** column.

3.  In the row containing the endpoint you want to delete, hover over the message type name, select
    the trash can that appears, and click **Delete**.

## See Also

- [Creating Message Types](partner-manager-create-message-type)
- [Modifying Message Types](modify-message-type-settings)
---

# Deleting Third-Party Connections

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you are no longer using a third-party connection, you can delete it from Anypoint Partner
Manager. However, you cannot delete third-party connections if you have configured or deployed
message flows for your partners that reference the third-party identifier or endpoints.

When you delete a third-party connection, it is removed from the **Partners** page. Also, all of the
connectionâs data is deleted, except for information about connection-related activities that
occurred before the deletion.

To delete a third-party connection:

1.  In Partner Manager, select **Partners** in the sidebar.
2.  Select the third-party connection to delete and click **Delete**.
3.  Confirm the deletion.

After you delete a third-party connection, you can reuse the connection name.

## See Also

- [Creating Third-Party Connections](create-third-party)
- [Modifying Third-Party Connection Settings](modify-third-party-settings)
---

# Deploying and Testing Message Flows

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Partner Manager dynamically validates the message flow configuration elements as you
complete them and displays a green checkmark next to each of the validated elements.

After Partner Manager validates the message flow configuration elements, deploy and test the message
flow in a sandbox environment. It can take up to five minutes from the time the message flow
deployment completes in the UI for the changes to take effect in the runtime.

## Deploy Message Flows

To deploy a message flow, click the **Deploy** button.

The message flow is deployed to Mule runtime engine, enabling you to:

- Start receiving messages from your partner.
- Use the **Activity** page to track the processed messages.

## Test a Deployed Message Flow

After you deploy the message flow, send test messages to verify that:

- You can receive messages and acknowledgments from your partners.
- The DataWeave map transformed the payload correctly.
- Your backend applications received the transformed messages.

Test your message flow in the Partner Manager sandbox environment by sending a test file to the
receiving endpoint:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Activity**.
2.  Search for the transmission by specifying the desired filters.
3.  In the transmission search results, you can do the following:
    - Verify that the transmission was sent or received.
    - Verify that Partner Manager sent the transmission to the correct endpoint.
    - If TA1 and functional acknowledgments (997) were set, review and confirm that they are present
      in the transmission.

4.  Click the transmission to view more summary information.

    Here, you can verify whether Partner Manager routed the transmission to the correct message
    flow. If the transmission involves AS2 endpoints, you can also verify whether the transmission
    sent a message disposition notification (MDN).

5.  Click the messages link to view summary information for the messages in the transmission and
    then click an individual message.
6.  Click **Target payload** in the **Target** section to view the messageâs payload and determine
    whether Partner Manager transformed the message correctly.
7.  Repeat steps 5-6 for each message to verify.

## See Also

- [Undeploying Message Flows](undeploy-message-flows)
- [Creating Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow)
---

# Deploying Partner Manager Runtime Apps

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can deploy Anypoint Partner Manager runtime apps to either CloudHub or Anypoint Runtime Fabric.

To configure Partner Manager deployment settings, use the **Partner Manager Settings** page in
access management. You can configure separate deployment settings for each business group
environment. You must have the `Partner Manager Administrator` permission to configure or modify
deployment settings.

## Deploy Partner Manager Runtime Apps to a New Environment

You must configure Partner Manager settings for a new environment if there is no Partner Manager
configuration for any environment in a particular business group:

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the `Organization Administrator`
    permission.
2.  On the main Anypoint Platform page, click **Access Management**.
3.  In the Access Management sidebar, click **Business Groups**.
4.  Click **Partner Manager** and then click **Configure new environment**.
5.  Select the environment and deployment target types.
6.  In the **Configure a Partner Manager environment** window, configure either
    [CloudHub 1.0 deployment settings](cloudhub-deployment-settings),
    [CloudHub 2.0 deployment settings](cloudhub2-deployment-settings), or
    [Runtime Fabric deployment settings](rtf-deployment-settings).

## Deploy Partner Manager Runtime Apps to an Existing Environment

Follow these steps to deploy Partner Manager to an existing business group environment:

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the `Organization Administrator`
    permission.
2.  On the main Anypoint Platform page, click **Access Management**.
3.  In the Access Management sidebar, click **Business Groups**.
4.  Click **Partner Manager**.
5.  In the **Configure a Partner Manager environment** window, configure
    [CloudHub 1.0 deployment settings](cloudhub-deployment-settings),
    [CloudHub 2.0 deployment settings](cloudhub2-deployment-settings), or
    [Runtime Fabric deployment settings](rtf-deployment-settings).

## Modify Partner Manager Deployment Settings

You can modify all Partner Manager deployment settings for an environment if you havenât deployed
any message flows to that environment.

If youâve deployed message flows to an environment, you can change the following settings:

- For CloudHub deployments, you can change all settings except for the deployment target (CloudHub
  to Runtime Fabric) and deployment region.
- For Runtime Fabric deployments, you can change all settings except for the deployment target
  (Runtime Fabric to CloudHub), application name prefix, and Anypoint MQ region.

To modify other deployment settings:

1.  Undeploy all message flows in the environment.
2.  Modify the deployment settings.
3.  Redeploy the message flows.

## See Also

- [Deploying and Testing Message Flows](deploy-message-flows)
- [Undeploying Message Flows](undeploy-message-flows)
---

# Deploying AS2, HTTP, and HTTPS Receive Endpoints

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can deploy AS2 receive, HTTP receive, and HTTPS receive endpoints on a public or private port.

## Deploy to CloudHub 1.0 on a Public Port

When an AS2 or HTTPS receive endpoint is deployed to CloudHub with the private port configuration
disabled, the endpoint application is deployed on the public HTTPS port (`8082`) or HTTP port
(`8081`), with the traffic routed through the
[shared load balancer](../../cloudhub/dedicated-load-balancer-tutorial). The value in the URL field
is automatically generated when you deploy a message flow that uses this endpoint.

You can view the runtime application name and external URL for a deployed endpoint from the endpoint
details page. For example:

![Example of a runtime application name and external URL](_images/pm-receive-endpoint-url.png)

You can share the external URL with the partners that send AS2, HTTP, or HTTPS messages to this
endpoint.

If your organization uses Anypoint Virtual Private Cloud without a dedicated load balancer (DLB),
your organizationâs platform administrator can configure the necessary
[Anypoint VPC firewall rules](../../cloudhub/vpc-firewall-rules-concept) to allow specific partner
or third-party connection IP addresses to access the endpoint.

## Deploy to CloudHub 1.0 on a Private Port

To deploy an AS2, HTTP, or HTTPS receive endpoint to CloudHub 1.0 with the private port
configuration enabled, you can:

- Deploy a message flow that includes the receive endpoint.
- Set CloudHub to automatically deploy newly created endpoints to a private port, as described in
  [Configuring CloudHub 1.0 Deployment Settings](cloudhub-deployment-settings).

Endpoints with the private port configuration enabled are deployed to the private HTTPS port `8092`
or HTTP (only for AS2) port `8091`. You can view the runtime application name, internal URL, and
external URL for a deployed endpoint from the endpoint details page, which you can access from the
<\*host\*> page (AS2 endpoints) or partner page (HTTP or HTTPS endpoints), or from a message flow.

Once an endpoint is deployed, follow these steps:

1.  Configure URL mapping rules to forward the messages received from your partners to an
    application, as described in the following sections and in
    [Dedicated Load Balancer Mapping Rules](../../cloudhub/lb-mapping-rules).
2.  Have your organizationâs platform administrator add your partnerâs IP addresses to a list of
    allowed addresses in the DLB setting, as described in
    [Dedicated Load Balancer Allowlists](../../cloudhub/lb-allowlists).

## Deploy to CloudHub 2.0

Partner Manager deploys AS2 and HTTP inbound endpoints on CloudHub 2.0 with a HTTPS listener.

- When using a shared space as the deployment target, the endpoints are accessible over the Internet
  and HTTP requests arenât allowed.
- When using a private space as the deployment target, you can use CloudHub 2.0 networking
  configurations, such as Ingress and Firewall rules, to manage and route incoming requests to your
  runtime applications.
  - If you want to allow HTTP requests to the AS2, HTTP, or HTTPS inbound endpoint application:
    1.  Use a private space as the deployment target.
    2.  On the **Advanced** tab, select `Accept HTTP` in the **HTTP requests** field.
    3.  Optionally, configure Network-Level Ingress Rules to allow HTTP requests from specific IP
        addresses.

## External URLs and Mapping Rules for Deployed Receive Endpoints

The external URLS for receive endpoints deployed to public or private ports vary depending on the
endpoint type, DLB domain, and input start_paths. You can share external URLs with your partners.

### External URL and Mapping Rules for an AS2 Receive Endpoint

The external URL for an AS2 receive endpoint is `https://{DLB-domain}/{Input-path}/receive-as2`.

For example, if the name of your DLB domain is `mythical.lb.anypointdns.net` and the runtime
application name is `b2b-inbound-as2-zap3`, your administrator can add a URL mapping rule in the DLB
settings to forward incoming requests to the runtime application within your Anypoint VPC:

![URL mapping rules for AS2 in the DLB settings](_images/url-mapping-rules-as2.png)

With this configuration, the external AS2 endpoint URL is
`https://mythical.lb.anypointdns.net/as2/mythical/receive-as2/`.

### External URL and Mapping Rule for an HTTPS Receive Endpoint

The external URL for an HTTPS endpoint is
`https://{DLB-domain}/{Input-path}/{base-path}/{message-type}`.

For example, if the name of your DLB domain is `mythical.lb.anypointdns.net`, the base path you
configured for the endpoint is `send2`, and the runtime application name is `b2b-inbound-http-ognq`,
then your administrator can add a URL mapping rule in the DLB settings to forward incoming requests
to the runtime application within your Anypoint VPC. For example:

![URL mapping rules for HTTPS in the DLB settings](_images/url-mapping-rules-https.png)

## Deploy to Runtime Fabric

Partner Manager deploys AS2 and HTTP inbound endpoints to Runtime Fabric with an HTTP listener. You
can expose HTTPS endpoints for internal and external users by using Runtime Fabric network
configurations and Ingress rules.

## See Also

- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
- [AS2 Receive Endpoint Settings](endpoint-as2-receive)
- [HTTP and HTTPS Receive Endpoint Settings](endpoint-https-receive)
---

# Message Types

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Message types define the structure of the transactions that Anypoint Partner Manager receives or
sends. You can define the following kinds of message types:

- Receive from Partners

  Defines the structure of the transactions that partners or third-party connections send to the
  host through inbound message flows

- Target at Host

  Defines the structure of the transactions that the host sends to the backend through inbound
  message flows

- Source at Host

  Defines the structure of the transactions that the backend sends to the host through outbound
  message flows

- Send to Partners

  Defines the structure of the transactions that the host sends to partners or third-party
  connections through outbound message flows

Message types are always managed on the **<host**\> page, regardless of whether they are used for a
single partner or for multiple partners. Additionally, message types are reusable across message
flows for different partners.

## See Also

- [Creating Message Types](partner-manager-create-message-type)
- [Receive from Partners Message Type](message-type-receive-from-partners)
- [Target at Host Message Type](message-type-target-at-host)
- [Source at Host Message Type](message-type-source-at-host)
- [Send to Partners Message Type](message-type-send-to-partners)
---

# EDI Acknowledgment Reconciliation

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

As your partners acknowledge your messages, Partner Manager automatically updates their
acknowledgment status. To be notified, you must specifically configure acknowledgment settings when
you create the message.

If you expect to receive functional acknowledgments from your partners, you must
[configure the acknowledgment settings](x12-send-settings) when you create the outbound message
flow.

When your organization receives the functional acknowledgment (X12 997 or EDIFACT CONTRL) from your
partner, Partner Manager updates the status of the corresponding outbound transaction to one of the
following:

- `Accepted`
- `Accepted with errors`
- `Partially accepted`
- `Rejected`
- `Overdue`, if your organization does not receive the functional acknowledgment within the
  specified time period

The following are examples of the actions your operations team might take while troubleshooting
acknowledgment issues:

- Retriggering the transaction from the back-end application when reviewing rejected transactions
  due to missing data elements
- Asking your partner to confirm receipt of the outbound transaction when reviewing overdue
  transactions

## Troubleshoot Rejected or Overdue Acknowledgments

You can view all the outbound transactions that have either received an X12 997 or EDIFACT CONTRL
rejection or that are overdue for the functional acknowledgment:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Activity**.
2.  Use the search filters to filter functional acknowledgments by:
    - **Type**  
      Type of message transmission:
      - **Both directions**
      - **Sent to partners**
      - **Received from partners**

    - **From Date**  
      Filter for transmissions sent or received after this date.
    - **To Date**  
      Filter for transmissions that are sent or received up to and including this date.
    - **Ack Status**  
      Filter for transmissions based on their acknowledgment status:
      - **All**
      - **Overdue**
      - **Rejected**

3.  Click the transaction you want to review.
4.  From the transaction detail page, in the **Transmission** section, expand the **Sent to
    <partner name>** section.
5.  Click **Review**.

    ![A transmission log that tracks all sent and received messages](_images/partner-manager-review-ack.png)

    When you review an X12 997 or EDIFACT CONTRL rejection, you see the X12 997 or EDIFACT CONTRL
    payload received from your partner. For example (for X12 997):

    ![X12 997 payload received from the partner](_images/partner-manager-997-ack-review-payload.png)

6.  Enter a review comment to indicate the action taken on the transactionâs acknowledgment status
    and then click **Mark reviewed**.
7.  Optionally, download the X12 payload by clicking **Download**.

After an overdue or rejected transaction is marked as reviewed, it is no longer displayed in the
transaction **Activity** page when you search for transactions with the acknowledgment status of
`Overdue` or `Rejected`. You see only the transactions that are not reviewed by your operations
team.

## See Also

- [Track Transmission Activity](activity-tracking)
- [Troubleshooting Anypoint Partner Manager](troubleshooting)
---

# Configuring Receive Settings for Inbound EDIFACT Messages

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Partner Manager receive settings include the EDIFACT acknowledgements and validation rules to apply
when processing an inbound EDIFACT message from the specified partner.

To configure EDIFACT receive settings:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Partners**.
2.  Select the partner to which these settings apply.
3.  In the **Validation and acknowledgement settings** sectionâs **Receive from <partner-name>**
    column, click **EDIFACT**.
4.  Select a link to configure:
    - [Acknowledgment settings](#ack-settings)
    - [Validation settings](#validation-settings)
    - [Control Numbers](#control-numbers)

5.  Click **Save**.

> [!NOTE] Your changes can take up to five minutes take effect on the runtime.

## Acknowledgment Settings

Optionally configure acknowledgment settings for each Inbound EDIFACT message flow by enabling the
**Send CONTRL functional acknowledgement when messages fail to route to a message flow** setting.

When there is a routing failure, this acknowledgment indicates that the message is rejected.

If you enable one of these options, either select the partner endpoint to which to send the
acknowledgments or create a new endpoint for this purpose.

## Validation Settings

Select one or more of the following options to ensure that all EDIFACT messages received from the
partner meet validation rules:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Enforce length limits</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects a message with values that are too short or long.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager accepts the message.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce value repeats</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects a message with values that are repeated too many or too few times.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager accepts the message.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce valid characters</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects a message that contains invalid characters.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, the invalid characters are either ignored or replaced, and Partner Manager accepts the message.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Allow unknown segments</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager ignores unknown segments.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager rejects a message that contains unknown segments.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce segment order</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager enables the segment to be reordered.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager rejects a message that contains segments that are out of order.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Allow Unused segments</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager ignores unused segments.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager rejects a message that contains unused segments.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce segment repeats</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects a message that contains segments that repeat too many times.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager accepts the message.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

If a message does not meet an enabled rule, Partner Manager rejects it.

Partner Manager logs all errors and reports them in the CONTRL acknowledgments.

## Control Numbers

Use these settings to apply validations related to your partner or to your use of control numbers
within the EDIFACT message:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Require unique interchange control number</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager records the interchange numbers previously processed and rejects duplicate interchange numbers from the same partner, as determined by the interchange sender and receiver identification.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager enables processing of the received interchange to continue.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Require unique message control number</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, Partner Manager enforces globally unique message control numbers (UNH01) for received transaction sets.</p></div><div><p>This configuration requires message control numbers to be unique across all EDIFACT messages received from the same partner and application, as determined by the interchange sender and receiver identification and application codes.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Creating Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Partners](create-partner)
- [Modify Partner Settings](modify-partner-settings)
---

# Configuring Send Settings for Outbound EDIFACT Messages

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Partner Manager enables you to configure the EDIFACT envelope parameters, validation rules, control
numbers, character encoding, and separators to apply when sending an outbound EDIFACT transmission
from the host to a partner.

To configure a partnerâs EDIFACT send settings:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Partners**.
2.  Select the partner for whom to configure the send settings.
3.  In the **Validation and acknowledgement settings** sectionâs **Send to <partner-name>** column,
    click **EDIFACT**.
4.  Select the setting to configure:
    - [General settings](#general-settings)
    - [Global control numbers](#global-control-numbers)
    - [Character set & delimiters](#character-set)

5.  Click **Save**.

The values in the partnerâs settings page are used by default for all EDIFACT messages sent to that
partner. You can override the settings you configure at the message flow level by selecting the
custom option while creating an outbound message flow.

> [!NOTE] Your changes can take up to five minutes to take effect on the runtime.

## General Settings

Use the following settings to configure the general validation rules for outbound EDIFACT messages:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Send Syntax Version</p></td><td><p>Syntax version for outbound messages</p></td><td><p>VERSION4</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send UNA</p></td><td><p>If enabled, adds a UNA segment at the beginning of the generated EDIFACT payload</p></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce valid characters</p></td><td><p>Enforces valid characters</p></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce length limits</p></td><td><p>Validates data elements against length limits defined in the EDI schema used</p></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expect CONTRL for each transmission sent to &lt;partner&gt;</p></td><td><p>When enabled, sets UNB-09 to <code>1</code>, indicating that a technical acknowledgment is expected in the partnerâs response</p></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce code set validations</p></td><td><p>If selected, Partner Manager enforces EDIFACT code set validations, as defined in the EDIFACT schemas.</p></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Global Control Numbers

Partner Manager applies a global control number at the partner level on the EDIFACT interchanges
generated from outbound message flows. These control numbers are sequenced across all message types
sent to a partner, including functional acknowledgments. The control number starts with a default
value of `1` and increases sequentially each time your organization sends outbound transmissions.

To change the initial value to maintain sequencing and avoid duplication:

1.  Click **Change** and enter the desired initial control number, up to 14 characters.
2.  Click **Save**.

\[NOTE\] Your changes can take up to five minutes to take effect on the runtime.

## Character Set and Delimiters

Use the following settings to set the character encoding and separators for the generated EDIFACT
payload:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Character encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>Character encoding used for messages.</p></div></div></td><td><p>ASCII_B</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Data element separator</p></td><td><p>Character used to separate data elements (can be a hex code).</p></td><td><p>+</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Component separator</p></td><td><p>Character used to separate components (can be a hex code).</p></td><td><p>:</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Repetition separator</p></td><td><p>Repetition separator character (can be a hex code).</p></td><td><p>*</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Segment terminator</p></td><td><p>Segment terminator character (can be a hex code).</p></td><td><p>'</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Release character</p></td><td><p>Character that acts as an escape for characters used as separators.</p></td><td><p>?</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Segment line ending</p></td><td><div><div><p>Character to use for line endings between segments to improve the readability of the output message text. Possible values include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p><div><p>Generates the EDIFACT payload as a single line with segments separated by this character</p></div></li><li><p>CR</p><div><p>Carriage return</p></div></li><li><p>CRLF</p><div><p>Carriage return and line feed</p></div></li><li><p>LF</p><div><p>Line Feed</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>CRLF</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test Indicator</p></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates if the EDIFACT transaction set is for testing or production.</p></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use segment line ending in last Line</p></td><td><p>If <code>Yes</code>, Partner Manager applies the segment line ending character to the last segment in the payload.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Creating Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Partners](create-partner)
- [Modify Partner Settings](modify-partner-settings)
---

# AS2 Receive Endpoint Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

AS2 receive endpoint settings determine where to receive B2B messages received over AS2.

An AS2 Receive from Partners endpoint (the only type of AS2 receive endpoint) acts as the source
endpoint in an inbound message flow that receives B2B messages from partners either directly or via
third-party connections. One AS2 endpoint can receive data from multiple partners, multiple
third-party connections, or both.

Only a host can own AS2 Receive from Partners endpoints.

> [!IMPORTANT] Each AS2 receive endpoint configuration gets deployed as an individual Mule
> application in the runtime. To minimize the number of vCores needed to run Anypoint Partner
> Manager, use an existing AS2 receive endpoint configuration across multiple message flows.

Create an AS2 Receive from Partners endpoint by configuring these settings on the **<host>** page:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Owner</p></td><td><p>Host that owns the endpoint.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td><p>Endpoint function (<code>Receive from partners</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>Protocol that the endpoint supports (<code>AS2</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint name</p></td><td><p>Automatically generated name used to identify the endpoint, which you can modify manually.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>User-supplied value that describes the purpose of the endpoint.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Private port (when deploying to VPC)</p></td><td><div><div><p>Applies when using Anypoint Virtual Private Cloud (Anypoint VPC) with CloudHub 1.0 deployments:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>When enabled, the AS2 receive endpoint application deploys to the private port <code>8092</code> in your <a href="../../cloudhub/virtual-private-cloud">Anypoint VPC</a>. You can manage the access control to the endpoint via the Anypoint VPC and dedicated load balancer settings.</p></li><li><p>When disabled, the AS2 receive endpoint application deploys to the public port <code>8082</code> by default.</p><div><p>You can access the endpoint URL directly over the internet.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>HTTPS</p></td><td><div><div><p>Applies to CloudHub 1.0 deployments.</p></div><div><p>Indicates whether Partner Manager deploys the AS2 receive endpoint as an HTTPS listener:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>If enabled (default), Partner Manager deploys the AS2 receive endpoint as an HTTPS listener.</p></li><li><p>If disabled, Partner Manager deploys the AS2 receive endpoint as an HTTP listener.</p></li></ul></div><div><p>The <strong>HTTPS</strong> option is disabled when creating Receive from Partners endpoints in CloudHub 2.0.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">When deploying to Anypoint Runtime Fabric, Partner Manager always deploys the AS2 receive endpoint as an HTTP listener. With this type of deployment, you must configure an Ingress controller to provide external consumers with an HTTPS endpoint. For more information, see <a href="../../runtime-fabric/latest/custom-ingress-configuration">Configuring Ingress for Runtime Fabric</a>.</div></div><div><div data-type="NOTE"><div><p>When deploying to CloudHub 2.0, Partner Manager always deploys the AS2 Receive endpoint as an HTTPS listener.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>When using a shared space as a deployment target, HTTP requests arenât allowed.</p></li><li><p>If you want to allow HTTP requests to the AS2 receive endpoint application:</p><div><ol><li><p>Use a private space as the deployment target.</p></li><li><p>On the <strong>Advanced</strong> tab, select <code>Accept HTTP</code> in the <strong>HTTP requests</strong> field.</p></li><li><p>Optionally, configure Network-Level Ingress Rules to allow HTTP requests from specific IP addresses.</p></li></ol></div></li></ul></div></div></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a id="manage-api-manager"></a>Manage with API Manager</p></td><td><div><div><p>Allows you to manage the endpoint in API Manager, where you can apply policies, such as authentication and client credentials, and configure SLA tiers. Document your message specifications by using Exchange and share them with your partners via public portals from Exchange or by obtaining an additional license and sharing the specifications in API Experience Hub.</p></div><div><p>To manage an endpoint in API Manager, create an HTTP API in API Manager in the same environment and organization as the endpoint.</p></div><div><p>When you create the API in API Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Select <code>HTTP API</code> as the asset type.</p></li><li><p>Leave the defaults on the <strong>Endpoint configuration</strong> screen.</p></li><li><p>Copy the API instance ID and paste it into the <strong>API Auto discovery id</strong> field in the Partner Manager <strong>New endpoint: Receive from Partners</strong> window.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use hyphen (-) as the secondary file name delimiter to determine non-EDI message type</p></td><td><p>Lets you use a hyphen as the secondary file name delimiter to determine the message type from the messageâs file name.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>AS2 URL</p></td><td><p>URL to the AS2 server (automatically generated).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce security level</p></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether Partner Manager rejects incoming AS2 messages that arenât signed, encrypted, or both. When Partner Manager rejects a message, it returns a negative Message Disposition Notification (MDN) to the sender.</p></div><div><p>Allowed values:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>Signed &amp; Encrypted</code></p><div><p>(Default) Rejects incoming messages that arenât both signed and encrypted.</p></div></li><li><p><code>Signed</code></p><div><p>Rejects incoming messages that arenât signed.</p></div></li><li><p><code>Encrypted</code></p><div><p>Rejects incoming messages that arenât encrypted.</p></div></li><li><p><code>None</code></p><div><p>Accepts messages that arenât signed or encrypted.</p></div></li></ul></div><div><p>When deploying an AS2 endpoint to CloudHub using the HTTP protocol, you cannot change the default value (<code>Signed &amp; Encrypted</code>).</p></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>App Name Substitution Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Defines how the ingress URL template <code>app-name</code> is substituted for this AS2 endpoint to generate the Runtime Fabric public endpoint.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Select Default to use <code>app-name</code> as deployment-name.</p></li><li><p>Select Custom to provide a custom name for the <code>app-name</code> substitution.</p></li><li><p>Select BasePath to use the editable AS2 base path as the value for the <code>app-name</code> substitution.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;Host&gt; keystore</p></td><td><div><div><p>Host keystore file that stores your organizationâs public certificate and private key. Select an existing keystore file or create a new one.</p></div><div><p>To create a new keystore file:</p></div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>&lt;host&gt; Keystore</strong> field, click <strong>Select</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>New</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Complete the fields on the <strong>New certficate for &lt;host&gt; window</strong>, as described in <a href="manage-as2-host-keystores">Managing AS2 Host Keystores</a>.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Create</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Follow these steps to add basic authentication for incoming AS2 messages from your partners:

1.  Configure the endpoint with [Manage with API Manager](#manage-api-manager).
2.  Configure a client credentials policy in API Manager and select **HTTP Basic Authentication
    Header** as the credentials origin.

    ![Configure client credentials policy in API Manager](_images/configure-client-credentials-policy.png)

3.  Add documentation to the HTTP API from Exchange and engage with your partners by publishing it
    to the [public portal](../../exchange/to-share-api-asset-to-portal) from Exchange or via API
    Experience Hub.
    - If you publish via a portal, your partners must log in and request access.
    - If you follow the
      [API request process](../../api-experience-hub/requesting-access-to-an-api-instance) on behalf
      of your partners, HTTP basic auth headers pass the client ID and client secret upon request.

## Next Step

After you create an AS2 receive endpoint, you can deploy it, as described in
[Deploying AS2, HTTP, and HTTPS Receive Endpoints](deploying-receive-endpoints).

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
- [AS2 Signature and Encryption Algorithms](as2-endpoints-algorithms)
- [Managing Host Certificates](manage-host-certificates)
---

# AS2 Send Endpoint Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

AS2 send endpoint settings determine where to send B2B messages transmitted over AS2.

An AS2 Send to Partners endpoint (the only type of AS2 send endpoint) can act as:

- A target endpoint in an outbound message flow through which transformed B2B messages are sent to
  partners, either directly or via third-party connections
- An acknowledgment endpoint in an inbound message flow

Either a partner or a third-party connection can own AS2 send endpoints.

Create AS2 endpoints by configuring these settings on the partner or third-party connection pages:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Owner</p></td><td><p>Partner or third-party connection that owns the endpoint.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td><p>Endpoint function (<code>Send to partners</code>)</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>Protocol that the endpoint supports (<code>AS2</code>)</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint name</p></td><td><p>Automatically generated name used to identify the endpoint, which you can subsequently modify manually.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>User-supplied value that describes the purpose of the endpoint.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;Host&gt; AS2 identifier</p></td><td><p>Your organizationâs AS2 identifier.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;Partner&gt; AS2 identifier</p></td><td><p>Your partnerâs AS2 identifier, which appears if the endpoint is used to send transactions directly to a partner without going through a third-party connection.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;VAN&gt; AS2 identifier</p></td><td><p>Your VANâs AS2 identifier, which appears if the endpoint is used to send transactions to a third-party connection.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Partner AS2 URL</p></td><td><p>Your partnerâs or third-party connectionâs AS2 URL to which outbound EDI transmissions are sent.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Certificate (TLS context)</p></td><td><div><div><p>(Applies when the <strong>Partner AS2 URL</strong> field starts with <code>https</code>) SSL certificate. Values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>Use CA-signed certificates</code></p><div><p>Uses a certificate that was issued and signed by a certificate authority (CA). With this option, you donât need to upload an additional certificate.</p></div></li><li><p><code>Use a self-signed certificate</code></p><div><p>Uses a certificate that was not issued or signed by a CA. With this option, you must either select an existing certificate or upload a new certificate in <code>.pem</code> format.</p></div></li><li><p><code>Allow insecure connection</code></p><div><p>Allows an insecure connection that doesnât perform certificate validation.</p></div><div><p>Donât use this option in production environments. Use it only during testing.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>x<a id="transfer-mode"></a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transfer Mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>The transfer mode applied when sending AS2 transmissions. Values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Auto</strong></p><div><p>Selected by default.</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Chunked Transfer Mode</strong></p><div><p>Always streaming.</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Identity Transfer Mode</strong></p><div><p>Never streaming.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection timeout</p></td><td><p>Timeout for establishing a connection to the remote service. The default is <code>30 Seconds</code>.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retry interval</p></td><td><p>Frequency of reconnection attempts when unable to connect to the remote AS2 service. The default is <code>30 Seconds</code>.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Maximum retry attempts</p></td><td><p>Maximum number of reconnection attempts to make. If you donât want Partner Manager to retry after an initial connection failure, set this value to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Respond to email address</p></td><td><p>Email address to use as the HTTP <code>From</code> header when sending.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message subject</p></td><td><p>User-defined field for the MIME Subject header.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filename pattern</p></td><td><p>Filename pattern required by your partner.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;Host&gt; keystore</p></td><td><div><div><p>Host keystore file that stores your organizationâs public certificate and private key. To create a new keystore when creating an endpoint:</p></div><div><div><div><ol><li><p>Click <strong>New</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Complete the fields on the <strong>New keystore for &lt;host&gt; window</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Upload Keystore</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></div><div><p>For more information about host keystores, see <a href="manage-as2-host-keystores">Managing AS2 Host Keystores</a>.</p></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encryption algorithm</p></td><td><p>Algorithm to use when encrypting the message to send.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Signature algorithm</p></td><td><p>Algorithm to use when setting the message-integrity check value.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request receipt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Whether you want the partner to return a Message Disposition Notification (MDN) receipt.</p></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MDN response</p></td><td><p>The AS2 messages sent from Partner Manager always request the receiver to send a Synchronous MDN receipt back. This value is always set to <code>Sync</code>.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MDN signature algorithm</p></td><td><p>Request for a partner to respond with a signed Message Disposition Notification (MDN) using this algorithm for calculating the Message Integrity Check (MIC).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
- [Filename Patterns for Send Endpoints](file-name-pattern)
- [AS2 Signature and Encryption Algorithms](as2-endpoints-algorithms)
- [Managing Host Certificates](manage-host-certificates)
---

# FTP Receive Endpoint Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

FTP receive endpoints determine where to receive B2B messages transmitted over FTP. There are two
types of FTP endpoints:

- Receive from Partners

  Source endpoint in an inbound message flow that receives B2B messages from partners either
  directly or via third-party connections.

  A host, partner, or third-party connection can own FTP Receive from Partners endpoints.

- Source at Host

  Source endpoint in an outbound message flow that receives messages from the backend applications.

  Only a host can own FTP Source at Host endpoints.

Create FTP receive endpoints by configuring these settings on the **<host>** or **Partner** pages.
You can also create these endpoints when you configure an outbound message flow:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Owner</p></td><td><p>Host, partner, or third-party connection that owns the endpoint.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td><p>Endpoint function (<code>Receive from partners</code> or <code>Source at host</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>Protocol that the endpoint supports (<code>FTP</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint Name</p></td><td><p>Automatically generated name used to identify the endpoint, which you can subsequently modify manually.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>User-supplied value that describes the purpose of the endpoint.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>FTP server host, such as <code>ftp.mulesoft.com</code>, <code>localhost</code>, or <code>192.168.0.1</code>. The host must be accessible from your Mule environment.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>Port number of the FTP server.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character encoding override</p></td><td><p>Enables inbound endpoints to receive non UTF-8 encoded documents.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Username for connecting to the FTP server.</p></td><td><p>x (if the server is authenticated)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Password for the FTP server.</p></td><td><p>x (if the server is authenticated)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source path</p></td><td><p>Path on the FTP server to which the partner or third-party connection sends the inbound files.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Skip Delete</p></td><td><div><div><p>When enabled, Partner Manager keeps the source file after reading and processing.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">Use this configuration only when connecting to SFTP/FTP hosts that donât allow explicit file deletion after the first read. Using this configuration with other SFTP/FTP hosts can result in duplicate file processing.</div></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Skip empty files</p></td><td><p>Skip empty files during processing.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Polling Frequency</p></td><td><p>Frequency with which the server checks the source path for new files. The default is <code>1 minute</code>.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supported Format Type</p></td><td><p>Applies only to FTP endpoints owned by partners or third-party connections. Indicates whether the endpoint supports EDI messages (EDIFACT, X12) or non-EDI messages (JSON, XML).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use hyphen (-) as the secondary file name delimiter to determine non-EDI message type</p></td><td><p>Lets you use a hyphen as the secondary file name delimiter to determine the message type from the messageâs file name. Only shows if the supported format type is non-EDI.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Size check wait time</p></td><td><p>Wait time, in seconds, between size checks to determine if a file is ready for processing. The default of <code>1 second</code> enables a file write to finish before processing the file.<br><br>Mule performs two size checks by waiting the amount of time specified in this field. If both checks return the same value, the file is ready to process.<br><br>To disable this feature, specify a negative number or omit the value.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Associated message type</p></td><td><p>(Applies when the format type is non-EDI) Message type to associate with the endpoint. If you select a message type, Partner Manager routes files received via the endpoint based on the selected message type instead of the filename.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Associated reference identifier</p></td><td><p>(Applies to partner and third-party endpoints when the format type is non-EDI) Partner information, such as the partner ID or third-party ID.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File name pattern</p></td><td><p>Filename pattern required by your partner.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Archival Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>FTP server path at which to store a copy of the file obtained from the source path.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The runtime application attempts to create the archive file up to three times. If permission or connection errors prevent Partner Manager from deleting the file, Partner Manager logs the issue in the runtime application and skips the archival step to prevent duplicate message processing.</div></div></div></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
---

# FTP Send Endpoint Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

FTP send endpoint settings configure where to send B2B messages transmitted over FTP. There are two
types of FTP send endpoints:

- Send to Partners
  - Target endpoint in an outbound message flow through which transformed B2B messages are sent to
    partners, either directly or through third-party connections
  - Acknowledgement endpoint in an inbound message flow

  A host, partner, or third-party connection can own FTP Send to Partners endpoints.

- Target at Host

  Target endpoint in an inbound message flow through which transformed B2B messages are sent to the
  backend applications

  Only a host can own FTP Target at Host endpoints.

Create FTP send endpoints by configuring these settings on the **<host>** or **Partner** pages. You
can also create these endpoints when you configure an outbound message flow:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Owner</p></td><td><p>Host, partner, or third-party connection that owns the endpoint.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td><p>Endpoint function (<code>Send to partners</code> or <code>Target at host</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>Protocol that the endpoint supports (<code>FTP</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint Name</p></td><td><p>Automatically generated name used to identify the endpoint, which you can modify manually.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>User-supplied value that describes the purpose of the endpoint.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retry interval (in seconds)</p></td><td><p>Frequency of reconnection attempts when unable to connect to the remote AS2 service. The default is <code>30 Seconds</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Maximum retry attempts</p></td><td><p>Maximum number of reconnection attempts to make. If you donât want Partner Manager to retry after an initial connection failure, set this value to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>FTP server host, such as <code>ftp.mulesoft.com</code>, <code>localhost</code>, or <code>192.168.0.1</code>. The host must be accessible from your Mule environment.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>Port number of the FTP.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Username for the FTP server.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Password for the FTP server.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Path</p></td><td><p>Path on your FTP server to EDI files that were generated from outbound message flows. This is also the path on your FTP server for storing transformed JSON and XML files generated from inbound message flows.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File name pattern</p></td><td><p>Filename pattern required by your partner or back-end application.</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
- [Filename Patterns for Send Endpoints](file-name-pattern)
---

# HTTP and HTTPS Receive Endpoint Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

HTTP and HTTPS receive endpoints configure where to receive B2B messages transmitted over HTTP or
HTTPS, respectively. There are two type of HTTP and HTTPS endpoints:

- Receive from Partners

  Source endpoint in an inbound message flow that receives inbound B2B messages from partners either
  directly or via third-party connections.

  A host, partner, or third-party connection can own HTTP and HTTPS Receive from Partners endpoints.

- Source at Host

  Source endpoint in an outbound message flow that receives application messages from the backend
  applications

  Only a host can own HTTP or HTTPS Source at Host endpoints.

> [!IMPORTANT] Each HTTP and HTTPS receive endpoint configuration gets deployed as an individual
> Mule application in the runtime. To minimize the number of vCores needed to run Anypoint Partner
> Manager, use an existing HTTP or HTTPS receive endpoint configuration across multiple message
> flows.

Create HTTP and HTTPS receive endpoints by configuring these settings on the **<host>** page:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Endpoint Name</p></td><td><p>Automatically generated name used to identify the endpoint, which you can modify manually.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Owner</p></td><td><div><div><p>Host, partner, or third-party connection that owns the endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td><p>Endpoint function (<code>Receive from partners</code> or <code>Source at host</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>Protocol that the endpoint supports (<code>HTTP</code> or <code>HTTPS</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>User-supplied value that describes the purpose of the endpoint.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Private port (when deploying to VPC)</p></td><td><div><div><p>Applies when using Anypoint Virtual Private Cloud (Anypoint VPC) with CloudHub deployments:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>When enabled, the AS2 receive endpoint application deploys to the private port <code>8092</code> in your <a href="../../cloudhub/virtual-private-cloud">Anypoint VPC</a>. You can manage the access control to the endpoint via the Anypoint VPC and dedicated load balancer settings.</p></li><li><p>When disabled, the AS2 receive endpoint application deploys to the public port <code>8082</code> by default.</p><div><p>You can access the endpoint URL directly over the internet.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Manage with API Manager</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enable this setting to manage the endpoint, including its access control, with API Manager. When you manage an endpoint with API Manager, you can apply various policies, including authentication and client credentials, and you can configure SLA tiers for the endpoint. You can also leverage Anypoint Exchange to document your message specifications and share them with your partners via public portals by leveraging Anypoint API Experience Hub. (This requires an additional license.)</p></div><div><p>To have an endpoint managed with API Manager, create an HTTP API in API Manager in the same environment and organization as the endpoint.</p></div><div><p>When you create this API in API Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Select <code>HTTP API</code> as the asset type.</p></li><li><p>Leave the defaults on the <strong>Endpoint configuration</strong> screen.</p></li><li><p>Copy the API instance ID and paste it into the <strong>API Auto discovery id</strong> field in the Partner Manager <strong>New endpoint: Receive from Partners</strong> window.</p></li></ul></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">Using this feature requires an API Manager license.</div></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP or HTTPS address where you receive outbound source messages from backend applications or inbound B2B messages from your partners. Partner Manager automatically generates this ID after the first message flow that uses the endpoint is deployed.</p></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response timeout (in milliseconds)</p></td><td><p>Response timeout period in milliseconds. The default is <code>15000</code>.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>App Name Substitution Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Defines how the ingress URL template <code>app-name</code> is substituted for this AS2 endpoint to generate the Runtime Fabric public endpoint.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Select Default to use <code>app-name</code> as deployment-name.</p></li><li><p>Select Custom to provide a custom name for the <code>app-name</code> substitution.</p></li><li><p>Select BasePath to use the editable AS2 base path as the value for the <code>app-name</code> substitution.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Authentication method used to protect the HTTP or HTTPS endpoint. This field is disabled when you manage the endpoint with API Manager.</p></div><div><p>Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>None</p><div><p>No authentication</p></div></li><li><p><a href="#api-key">API key</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#basic">Basic</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE]
>
> - When deploying to Anypoint Runtime Fabric, Partner Manager deploys the HTTP or HTTPS receive
>   endpoint as an HTTP listener.
>   - You must configure an Ingress controller to provide external consumers with an HTTPS endpoint.
>     For more information, see
>     [Configuring Ingress for Runtime Fabric](../../runtime-fabric/latest/custom-ingress-configuration).
>
> - When deploying to CloudHub 2.0, Partner Manager deploys the HTTP or HTTPS Receive endpoint as an
>   HTTPS listener.
>   - When using a shared space as a deployment target, HTTP requests arenât allowed.
>   - If you want to allow HTTP requests to the HTTP or HTTPS receive endpoint application:
>     1.  Use a private space as the deployment target.
>     2.  On the **Advanced** tab, select `Accept HTTP` in the **HTTP requests** field.
>     3.  Optionally, configure Network-Level Ingress Rules to allow HTTP requests from specific IP
>         addresses.

## API Key Authentication

Authentication type that requires a valid API key in the HTTP request header.

Use the following settings to configure API Key authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>HTTP Header Name</p></td><td><p>HTTP header parameter associated with the API key value.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><p>Value of the API key.</p><p>After saving your new or reconfigured endpoint, you cannot subsequently retrieve or modify the API key.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Certificate</p></td><td><p>Certificate to use for authentication.</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Basic Authentication

Authentication type that requires a username and password in the HTTP or HTTP request header.

Use the following settings to configure Basic authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Username to use when making a request.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Password to use when making a request.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Next Step

After you create an HTTP or HTTPS receive endpoint, you can deploy it, as described in
[Deploying AS2, HTTP, and HTTPS Receive Endpoints](deploying-receive-endpoints).

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
- [Managing Partner and Third-Party Connection Certificates](manage-partner-certificates)
---

# HTTP and HTTPS Send Endpoint Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

HTTP and HTTPS send endpoint settings configure where to send B2B messages transmitted over HTTP or
HTTPS, respectively. There are two types of HTTP and HTTPS send endpoints:

- Send to Partners
  - Target endpoint in an outbound message flow through which transformed B2B messages are sent to
    the partners either directly or via a third-party connection
  - Acknowledgement endpoint in an inbound message flow

  A host, partner, or third-party connection can own HTTP and HTTPS Send to Partners endpoints.

- Target at Host

  Target endpoint in an inbound message flow through which transformed messages are sent to the
  backend applications.

  Only a host can own HTTP and HTTPS Target at Host endpoints.

Create HTTP and HTTPS send endpoints by configuring these settings on the host, partner, or
third-party connection page. You can also create these endpoints when you configure an outbound
message flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Owner</p></td><td><p>Host, partner, or third-party connection that owns the endpoint.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td><p>Endpoint function (<code>Send to partners</code> or <code>Target at host</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>Protocol that the endpoint supports (<code>HTTP</code> or <code>HTTPS</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint Name</p></td><td><p>Automatically generated name used to identify the endpoint, which you can subsequently modify manually.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>User-supplied value that describes the purpose the endpoint.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>URL</p></td><td><p>Target HTTP or HTTPS service to which to send transformed X12, EDIFACT, JSON, XML, or CSV messages. The URL must be accessible from the runtime environment that processes your B2B integrations. Enter a well-formed URL (including the path) that specifies where to send the request, for example: <code>https://www.mulesoft.com/sendRequest</code></p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout (in milliseconds)</p></td><td><p>Maximum time, in milliseconds, for the request element to block the execution of a flow that is waiting for the HTTP response. The default is 15000 milliseconds.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retry interval (in seconds)</p></td><td><p>Frequency of reconnection attempts when unable to connect to the remote HTTP or HTTPS service. The default is 60 seconds.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Maximum retry attempts</p></td><td><p>Maximum number of reconnection attempts. To prevent Partner Manager from retrying after an initial connection failure, set this value to <code>0</code>. The default is <code>3</code>.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Authentication method used to protect the HTTP or HTTPS endpoint. Values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>None</p><div><p>No authentication</p></div></li><li><p><a href="#api-key">API key</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#basic">Basic</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#client-credentials">Client Credentials</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#oauth-token">OAuth Token</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Certificate (TLS Context)</p></td><td><div><div><p>(Applies to HTTPS endpoints) SSL certificate. Values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Use CA-signed certificates</p><div><p>Uses a certificate that was issued and signed by a certificate authority (CA). With this option, you donât need to upload an additional certificate.</p></div></li><li><p>Use a self-signed certificate</p><div><p>Uses a certificate that was not issued or signed by a CA. With this option, you must either select an existing certificate or upload a new certificate in <code>&lt;certifcate name&gt;.PEM</code> format.</p></div></li><li><p>Allow insecure connection</p><div><p>Allows an insecure connection that doesnât perform certificate validation.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## API Key Authentication

Authentication type that requires a valid API key in the HTTP request header.

Use the following settings to configure API Key authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>HTTP Header Name</p></td><td><p>HTTP header parameter associated with the API key value.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>API Key</p></td><td><p>Value of the API key.</p><p>After saving your new or reconfigured endpoint, you cannot subsequently retrieve or modify the API key.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Certificate</p></td><td><p>Certificate to use for authentication.</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Basic Authentication

Authentication type that requires a username and password in the HTTP or HTTP request header.

Use the following settings to configure Basic authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Username to use when making a request.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Password to use when making a request.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Client Credentials Authentication

Authentication type that requires a valid client ID and client secret in the HTTP or HTTPS request
header.

Use the following settings to configure Credit Credentials authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Client ID header name</p></td><td><p>HTTP header name that the client ID uses to call the endpoint URL. Default is <code>"client_id"</code>.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client ID header value</p></td><td><p>HTTP header value for the client ID.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client secret header name</p></td><td><p>HTTP header name for the client secret. Default is <code>"client_secret"</code>.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client secret header value</p></td><td><p>HTTP header value for the client secret.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth Token Authentication

Authentication type that requires a valid OAuth token in the HTTP or HTTPS request header.

Use the following settings to configure OAuth Token authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Token URL</p></td><td><p>URL of the authentication server that provides access tokens.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client ID</p></td><td><p>Client ID provided when registering your application with the authorization server.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><p>Client secret provided when registering your application with the authorization server.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scope</p></td><td><p>Permissions granted to a client application. Enter values as a space-separated list, where each string represents a permission requested by the client application. The field only applies to HTTP endpoints.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
- [Managing Partner and Third-Party Connection Certificates](manage-partner-certificates)
---

# SFTP Receive Endpoint Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

SFTP receive endpoints configure where to receive B2B messages transmitted over SFTP. There are two
types of SFTP receive endpoints:

- Receive from Partners

  Source endpoint in an inbound message flow that receives B2B messages from partners either
  directly or via third-party connections.

  A host, partner, or third-party connection can own SFTP Receive from Partners endpoints.

- Source at Host

  Source endpoint in an outbound message flow that receives messages from the backend applications.

  Only a host can own SFTP Source at Host endpoints.

Create SFTP receive endpoints by configuring these settings on the **<host>** or **Partner** page:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Owner</p></td><td><p>Partner or host that owns the endpoint.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td><p>Endpoint function (<code>Receive from partners</code> or <code>Source at host</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>Protocol that the endpoint supports (<code>SFTP</code>).</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint Name</p></td><td><p>Automatically generated name used to identify the endpoint, which you can subsequently modify manually.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>User-supplied value that describes the purpose of the endpoint.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>SFTP server host, such as <code>sftp.mulesoft.com</code>, <code>localhost</code>, or <code>192.168.0.1</code>. The host must be accessible from your Mule environment.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>Port number of the SFTP server.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character encoding override</p></td><td><p>Enables inbound endpoints to receive non UTF-8 encoded documents for XML, CSV, and X12 document types.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Authentication method used to secure the SFTP endpoint. Values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><strong>None</strong></p><div><p>Provides no authentication for the endpoint</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Basic</strong></p><div><p>Authenticates by using a username and password</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Identity Key</strong></p><div><p>Authenticates by using a private SSH identity key</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Basic and Identity Key</strong></p><div><p>Authenticates by using a username, password, and public SSH identity key</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>(Applies to all authentication types except <code>None</code>)</p><p>Username for the SFTP server.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>(Applies to these authentication types: <code>Basic</code>, <code>Basic and Identity Key</code>)</p><p>Password for the SFTP server.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SSH</p></td><td><div><div><p>(Applies to these authentication types: <code>Identity Key</code>, <code>Basic and Identity Key</code>)</p></div><div><p>SSH private key file. The following considerations apply:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Send and receive endpoints that have the same owner can use the same SSH private key file.</p></li><li><p>Multiple message flows for the same or different partners can reference endpoints that use the same SSH private key file.</p></li></ul></div><div><p>You can either select a private key file (<code>.pem</code>) thatâs already configured in Anypoint Partner Manager or create a new configuration for a private key file.</p></div><div><p>To create a new configuration for a private key file:</p></div><div><ol><li><p>In the <strong>&lt;partner&gt; SSH</strong> or <strong>&lt;host&gt; SSH</strong> field, click <strong>Select</strong>.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Select a &lt;partner&gt; ssh</strong> or <strong>Select a &lt;host&gt; ssh</strong> dialog, click <strong>New</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Complete the fields in the <strong>New certificate for &lt;partner&gt;</strong> or <strong>New certificate for &lt;host&gt;</strong> dialog:</p><div><ul><li><p><strong>Identity Key name</strong></p><div><p>Enter the name of the private key file.</p></div></li><li><p><strong>&lt;partner&gt; identity key PEM file</strong> or <strong>&lt;host&gt; identity key PEM file</strong></p><div><p>Click <strong>Choose file</strong> to select the private key file.</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Key passphrase</strong></p><div><p>Enter the passphrase for the private key file.</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Expiration date</strong></p><div><p>Enter the date on which you want the private key file to expire. This date is a reminder for you, but Anypoint Partner Manager doesnât enforce it.</p></div></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Click <strong>Create</strong>.</p></li></ol></div></div></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source path</p></td><td><p>Path on the SFTP server to which your partner sends the inbound files.</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Skip Delete</p></td><td><div><div><p>When enabled, Partner Manager keeps the source file after reading and processing.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">Use this configuration only when connecting to SFTP/FTP hosts that donât allow explicit file deletion after the first read.
