# Source at Host Message Type Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The Source at Host message type defines the way in which your backend applications transform
outbound transactions and send them to your organization.

You can define the CSV, JSON, or XML formats for the Source at Host message type.

## CSV

Use the following settings to define CSV as the format for the Source at Host message type:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td><p>Message usage. Allowed value is <code>Source at Host</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Format</p></td><td><p>Format of the message. Allowed value is <code>CSV</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Separator character</p></td><td><p>Character that separates values in the file.</p></td><td><p>,</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quote character</p></td><td><p>Character used as a quote character in the file.</p></td><td><p>""</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Escape character</p></td><td><p>Character used as an escape character in the file.</p></td><td><p>\</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Header line</p></td><td><p>When selected, Partner Manager skips the header row when it processes a CSV file.</p></td><td><p>Selected</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore empty line</p></td><td><p>When selected, Partner Manager ignores empty lines when it processes a CSV file.</p></td><td><p>Selected</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reference identifiers and attributes mapping</p></td><td><div><div><p>Uploaded DataWeave map that extracts the reference identifiers and custom attributes from the payload received from the partner:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>The extracted reference identifier values are used for dynamic routing into the appropriate message flow.</p></li><li><p>The extracted custom attributes provide visibility into the lifecycle of your B2B workflow. For more information, see <a href="use-custom-attributes">Using Custom Messaging Attributes with Message Types</a>.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## JSON or XML

Use the settings in the table to define JSON or XML as the format for the Receive from Partners
message type.

If you process cXML messages with <DOCTYPE> tags, you must set the `minMuleVersion` to 4.5.0 or
higher and upgrade your runtime environment. For more information, see Partner Manager deployment
settings for [CloudHub](cloudhub-deployment-settings),
[CloudHub 2.0](cloudhub2-deployment-settings), and [Runtime Fabric](rtf-deployment-settings).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Application message structure format. Allowed value is <code>JSON</code> or <code>XML</code>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Schema</p></td><td><p>Imported JSON or XSD schema file for the message type.</p></td><td></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message type identifier</p></td><td><div><div><p>See <a href="outbound-message-routing">Outbound Message Routing</a> to understand how Partner Manager performs outbound routing and how your backend applications pass the message type identifier when sending application messages to Partner Manager endpoints.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reference identifiers and attributes mapping</p></td><td><div><div><p>Uploaded DataWeave map that extracts the reference identifiers, business key, and custom message attributes from the payload received from the back-end application:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>The extracted reference identifier values are used for dynamic routing into the appropriate message flow.</p></li><li><p>The extracted custom attributes enable you to have visibility into the lifecycle of your B2B workflow. For more information, see <a href="use-custom-attributes">Using Custom Messaging Attributes with Message Types</a>.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Message Types](document-types)
- [Creating Message Types](partner-manager-create-message-type)
- [Deleting Message Types](delete-message-types)
---

# Target at Host Message Type

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The Target at Host message type defines the way in which Anypoint Partner Manager transforms inbound
transactions sent from your organization to your backend applications.

You can define the CSV, JSON, or XML format for the Target at Host message type.

## CSV

Use the following settings to define CSV as the format for the Target at Host message type:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td><p>Message usage. Allowed value is <code>Target at Host</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Format</p></td><td><p>Format of the message. Allowed value is <code>CSV</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## JSON or XML

Use the settings in the table to define JSON or XML as the format for the Receive from Partners
message type.

If you process cXML messages with <DOCTYPE> tags, you must set the `minMuleVersion` to 4.5.0 or
higher and upgrade your runtime environment. For more information, see Partner Manager deployment
settings for [CloudHub](cloudhub-deployment-settings),
[CloudHub 2.0](cloudhub2-deployment-settings), and [Runtime Fabric](rtf-deployment-settings).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Format of the message. Allowed value is <code>JSON</code> or <code>XML</code>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Schema</p></td><td><p>Imported JSON or XSD schema file for the message type.</p></td><td></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>Message type name that Partner Manager automatically generates:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>If a schema is associated with the message type, Partner Manager automatically generates the message type name from the schema filename.<br><br>You can modify this value.</p></li><li><p>If a schema is not associated with the message type, you must enter the message type name manually.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Message Types](document-types)
- [Creating Message Types](partner-manager-create-message-type)
- [Deleting Message Types](delete-message-types)
---

# Modifying Endpoints

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although endpoint settings donât often require changes, you might need to modify them under certain
circumstances. For example:

- Customers move their data center, so you must change the IP address or host name.
- Customers update their credentials, so you must change the user credentials in the endpoint
  configuration.
- Customers change their file naming conventions and response timeout requirements, so you must
  change these configurations for the endpoint.
- The downstream API that receives translated inbound data moves from one region to another, so you
  must change the endpoint URL.

To modify an endpoint:

1.  Access the endpoint you want to modify from the endpoint ownerâs page (the **<host>**,
    **<partner>**, or third-party page, depending on the endpoint).
2.  Click **Edit**.
3.  Modify the desired settings. What you can modify depends on the endpoint protocol and usage, as
    described in the following sections:
    - [AS2 Receive Endpoints](#as2-receive)
    - [AS2 Send Endpoints](#as2-send)
    - [FTP Receive Endpoints](#ftp-receive)
    - [FTP Send Endpoints](#ftp-send)
    - [SFTP Receive Endpoints](#sftp-receive)
    - [SFTP Send Endpoints](#sftp-send)
    - [HTTP Receive Endpoints](#http-receive)
    - [HTTP Send Endpoints](#http-send)
    - [HTTPS Receive Endpoints](#https-receive)
    - [HTTPS Send Endpoints](#https-send)

4.  Click **Save** to save your changes.
5.  On the confirmation window that opens, select the **I understand the effects of these actions**
    checkbox and then click **Confirm**.

    Partner Manager displays a **Deploy** button next to the modified endpoint.

6.  Click **Deploy** to keep the endpoint changes.
7.  On the confirmation window that opens, select the **I understand the effects of these actions**
    checkbox and then click **Confirm**.

    Partner Manager redeploys the endpoint and all message flows that use the endpoint.

    If required, Partner Manager also automatically deploys the Mule runtime applications that
    Partner Manager uses. The deployment of Mule runtime applications can take up to five minutes or
    longer to complete, depending on how many runtime applications are affected.

## AS2 Receive Endpoints

You can modify the following fields for an AS2 Receive from Partners endpoint:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Redeploys the Runtime Application for the Endpoint</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Endpoint name</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce security level and associated fields</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## AS2 Send Endpoints

You can modify the following fields for an AS2 Send to Partners endpoint:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Redeploys the Runtime Application for the Endpoint</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Authentication type and associated fields</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection timeout</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encryption algorithm</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint name</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filename pattern</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;host&gt; AS2 identifier</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;host&gt; keystore</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Maximum retry attempts</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MDN signature algorithm</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message subject</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;partner&gt; AS2 identifier</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;partner&gt; AS2 URL, except for changing <code>HTTP</code> to <code>HTTPS</code> or vice versa</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request receipt</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Respond to email address</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retry interval</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Signature algorithm</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## FTP Receive Endpoints

You can modify the following fields for an FTP Receive from Partners or Source at Host endpoint:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Redeploys the Runtime Application for the Endpoint</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Archival path</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint name</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File name pattern</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Polling frequency</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character encoding override</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Size check wait time</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source path</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## FTP Send Endpoints

You can modify the following fields for an FTP Send to Partners or Target at Host endpoint:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Redeploys the Runtime Application for the Endpoint</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint name</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File name pattern</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target path</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## SFTP Receive Endpoints

You can modify the following fields for an SFTP Receive from Partners or Source at Host endpoint:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Redeploys the Runtime Application for the Endpoint</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Endpoint name</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character encoding override</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication type</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SSH</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source path</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Polling frequency</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Size check wait time</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File name pattern</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Archival path</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## SFTP Send Endpoints

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Redeploys the Runtime Application for the Endpoint</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Endpoint name</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File name pattern</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication type</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SSH</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target path</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filename pattern</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

# Modifying Message Types

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can modify a message typeâs settings, but what you can modify depends on the message typeâs
usage and protocol:

1.  In the sidebar, select the **<host-name>** page.
2.  Locate the message type you want to modify.
3.  Change any of the following settings in the **Edit message type** window:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Message Type</th><th>Format</th><th>What You Can Modify</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>Receive from Partners</p></td><td><p>EDIFACT or X12</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Select a different schema file, if the <strong>Schema</strong> field is set to <strong>Customize</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a different reference map file to map the reference identifiers, business key, and custom attributes.</p></li><li><p>Add new custom message attributes.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>JSON or XML</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Select a different schema file.</p></li><li><p>Select a different reference map file to map the reference identifiers, business key, and custom attributes.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send to Partners</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td><td><p>Select a different schema file, if the <strong>Schema</strong> field is set to <strong>Customize</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>Source at Host</p></td><td><p>EDIFACT or X12</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Select a different schema file, if the <strong>Schema</strong> field is set to <strong>Customize</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a different reference map file to map the reference identifiers, business key, and custom attributes.</p></li><li><p>Add new custom message attributes.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>JSON or XML</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Select a different schema file.</p></li><li><p>Select a different reference map file to map the reference identifiers, business key, and custom attributes.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target at Host</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td><td><p>Select a different schema file, if the <strong>Schema</strong> field is set to <strong>Customize</strong>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  To dissociate an associated custom message attribute from a Receive from Partners or Source at
    Host message type, remove it from the associated reference map.

## See Also

- [Creating Message Types](partner-manager-create-message-type)
- [Creating Reference Maps for Message Types](create-reference-map)
- [Deleting Message Types](delete-message-types)
---

# Modifying Partner Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can modify a partnerâs settings including contact details, identifiers, digital certificates,
acknowledgments, control numbers, and more.

To modify a partnerâs settings:

1.  In Partner Manager, select **Partners** in the sidebar.
2.  Click the partner.
3.  Change any of the following settings:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Settings</th><th>Actions</th><th>Additional Information</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Partner information, including a partnerâs address, website, and contact details</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Modify partner information</p><div><p>Click <strong>Edit</strong> in the <strong>About</strong> section, click the contact type, and make the desired modifications.</p></div></li><li><p>Delete a contact</p><div><p>Click <strong>Edit</strong> in the <strong>About</strong> section, click the contact type, and click the <strong>Delete contact</strong> link.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Identifiers</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Add a new identifier</p><div><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Identifiers</strong> section and enter the required fields.</p></div></li><li><p>Delete an identifier</p><div><p>Select the identifier and click <strong>Delete</strong>.</p></div><div><p>Because a partner must have at least one identifier, you canât delete the last one.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><a href="partner-manager-identifiers">Sender and Receiver Identifiers</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>The partnerâs digital certificates, such as X.509 certificates</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Certificates</strong> section.</p></td><td><p><a href="manage-partner-certificates">Managing Partner and Third-Party Connection Certificates</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoints</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Create a new endpoint</p><div><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Endpoints</strong> section and enter the required fields.</p></div></li><li><p>Delete an endpoint</p><div><p>Click the endpointâs link and click <strong>Delete</strong>.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><a href="create-endpoint">Creating Endpoints</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Acknowledgements, validations, control numbers, character sets (X12 only), and separators (X12 send format only).</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Modify</p><div><p>Click the appropriate link in the <strong>Validation and acknowledgement settings</strong> section, and make the desired modifications.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="edifact-receive-read-settings">EDIFACT Receive Settings</a></p></li><li><p><a href="edifact-send-settings">EDIFACT Send Settings</a></p></li><li><p><a href="x12-receive-read-settings">X12 Receive Settings</a></p></li><li><p><a href="x12-send-settings">X12 Send Settings</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character encoding override option for received AS2 messages.</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Modify</p><div><p>Click <strong>AS2</strong> in <strong>Global endpoint settings</strong>.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><a href="as2-encoding">AS2 Receive Character Encoding Override Settings</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Configuring and Managing Partners](configure-partner)
- [Creating Partners](create-partner)
- [Deleting Partners](partner-manager-delete-partner)
---

# Modifying Third-Party Connection Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can modify third-party connection settings including contact details, identifiers, and digital
certificates.

To modify third-party connection settings:

1.  In Partner Manager, select **Partners** in the sidebar.
2.  Click the connection to modify.
3.  Modify any of the following settings:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Settings</th><th>Action</th><th>Additional Information</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Third-party company information, including the companyâs address, website, and contact details</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Modify partner information:</p><div><p>Click <strong>Edit</strong> in the <strong>About</strong> section, click the contact type, and make the desired modifications.</p></div></li><li><p>Delete a contact</p><div><p>Click <strong>Edit</strong> in the <strong>About</strong> section, click the contact type, and click the <strong>Delete contact</strong> link.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Identifiers</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Add a new identifier</p><div><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Identifiers</strong> section and enter the required fields.</p></div></li><li><p>Delete an identifier:</p><div><p>Select the identifier and click <strong>Delete</strong>.</p></div><div><p>Because a third-party connection must have at least one identifier, you canât delete the last one.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><a href="partner-manager-identifiers">Sender and Receiver Identifiers</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>The partnerâs digital certificates, such as X.509 certificates</p></td><td><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Certificates</strong> section.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoints</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Create a new connection-owned endpoint</p><div><p>Click <strong>New</strong> in the <strong>Endpoints</strong> section and enter the required fields.</p></div></li><li><p>Delete a connection-owned endpoint</p><div><p>Click the endpointâs link and click <strong>Delete</strong>.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><a href="create-endpoint">Creating Endpoints</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Creating Third-Party Connections](create-third-party)
- [Deleting Third-Party Connections](delete-third-party)
---

# Outbound Message Flows

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

An outbound message flow receives application messages from the backend, transforms the messages to
the format your partner supports, and then forwards the messages to your partner.

The following diagram shows the components of an outbound message flow:

![A flowchart showing how outbound messages are configured](_images/pm-outbound-message-flow.png)

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Callout</th><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Supported Formats or Protocols</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-1.png" alt="A circle with a number 1 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Receiving endpoint</p></td><td><p>Endpoint where the host receives transmissions from the backend</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>FTP</p></li><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li><li><p>SFTP</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-2.png" alt="A circle with a number 2 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Source message type</p></td><td><p>Message type of the message received from the backend</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>CSV</p></li><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>XML</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-3.png" alt="A circle with a number 3 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Transform</p></td><td><p>DataWeave map used to transform the JSON or XML formats to EDIFACT or X12. The map must have Java output (<code>output application/java</code>) to transform JSON or XML formats to EDIFACT.</p></td><td><div><div><p>N/A</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-4.png" alt="A circle with a number 4 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Target message type</p></td><td><p>Message type to which to transform the input message</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>CSV</p></li><li><p>EDIFACT</p></li><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>X12</p></li><li><p>XML</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-5.png" alt="A circle with a number 5 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Target endpoint</p></td><td><p>Partner endpoint to which to send the transformed message</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>AS2</p></li><li><p>FTP</p></li><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li><li><p>SFTP</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Creating Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow)
- [Deploying and Testing Message Flows](deploy-message-flows)
- [Modifying Message Flow Settings](manage-message-flows)
- [Outbound Message Routing](outbound-message-routing)
---

# Outbound Message Routing

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The Anypoint Partner Manager outbound message routing framework enables you to implement receiving
endpoints in a variety of ways. You can:

- Use a single endpoint to receive different types of application messages, such as advance shipment
  notices and invoices, from your backend systems.
- Use dedicated endpoints for each message type from backend systems.
- Follow a balanced approach by implementing a mixed set of receive endpoints to receive application
  messages from backend systems.

When messages are received by the endpoint, Partner Manager routes the payload to the appropriate
message flow based on the message type, partner reference ID, and host reference ID configurations:

1.  Identifies the message type using the following criteria:
    - Relative path for HTTP or HTTPS endpoints
    - Filename for SFTP endpoints, which follow this convention:

      `<message-type>_<endpoint-information>.<file-type>`.

      For example, if your message type ID is ar-invoice, then the filename is
      `ar-invoice_20210217_1055_000.json`.

2.  Validates the payload against the schema supplied during the message type definition.
3.  Uses the supplied DataWeave map to obtain the partner reference ID, optional host reference IDs,
    and the business key.

    The partner reference ID and host reference ID specify the message flow through which the
    transaction is processed.

![A flowchart showing how outbound messages are routed](_images/pm-outbound-message-routing.png)

## See Also

- [Deploying and Testing Message Flows](deploy-message-flows)
- [Modifying Message Flow Settings](manage-message-flows)
- [Track Transmission Activity](activity-tracking)
---

# Partner Manager Administration

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Administering Partner Manager involves tracking the activity of transmissions and individual
messages, reconciling EDI acknowledgments, and upgrading message flow runtime templates.

For information about assigning permissions, see
[Getting Started with Partner Manager](get-started).
---

# Partner Manager Deployment Architecture

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Understanding how Partner Manager deploys runtime applications helps you choose the right deployment
target for your B2B integrations. The runtime architecture differs between CloudHub and Runtime
Fabric, though the configuration experience remains the same.

When you deploy a message flow, Partner Manager automatically transforms your configurations and
deploys runtime applications to orchestrate end-to-end B2B processing. These applications include
receive endpoint services for inbound messages, document processing for parsing and translation,
replication service for transaction tracking, and send endpoint services for delivering messages to
partners or internal systems.

![A flowchart showing how data moves between trading partners and backend systems](_images/pm-high-level-arch.png)

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Callout</th><th>Name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-1.png" alt="A circle with a number 1 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Partner Manager</p></td><td><p>MuleSoft-hosted cloud control plane enabling users to configure partner profiles, build and deploy B2B integration message flows, and monitor B2B transaction activity</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-2.png" alt="A circle with a number 2 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Receive Endpoint Services</p></td><td><p>Services to receive inbound B2B or EDI messages from partner transport protocols such as AS2 and SFTP or outbound application messages from your organizationâs backend systems via HTTP, HTTPS, or SFTP</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-3.png" alt="A circle with a number 3 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Document Processing Service</p></td><td><p>Core B2B processing engine responsible for parsing, validating, and translating received B2B messages based on configurations and mapping in Partner Manager</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-4.png" alt="A circle with a number 4 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>API Replication Service</p></td><td><p>Tracking service responsible for updating transmissions and transaction activities to the control plane and the payload storage API</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-5.png" alt="A circle with a number 5 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Send Endpoint Services</p></td><td><p>Services to deliver transformed inbound application messages to internal applications such as ERP, billing, order management via transport protocols like HTTP, HTTPS, or SFTP; or outbound B2B EDI payloads to your partners via AS2, HTTP, HTTPS, or SFTP</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/pm-annotation-6.png" alt="A circle with a number 6 inside"></span></p></td><td><p>Payload Storage API</p></td><td><p>API to store B2B payload data in your storage solution</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Partner Manager for CloudHub 1.0 or 2.0

When deploying B2B integration message flows to CloudHub 1.0, CloudHub 2.0, Anypoint MQ uses queues
between processing stages to add additional reliability to B2B message processing. Anypoint MQ
generates queues automatically in the same or nearest available region during the deployment
process.

Runtime applications for environments configured to deploy to CloudHub 1.0 deploy to the CloudHub
region configured in the environment settings in Access Management.

Runtime applications for environments configured to deploy to CloudHub 2.0 deploy to the shared or
private space configured in the environment settings in Access Management.

New runtime applications deploy to the same region as the first deployment.

![A diagram that illustrates how data moves through CloudHub](_images/cloudhub-deployment.png)

## Partner Manager for Runtime Fabric

When deploying message flows to Runtime Fabric, Partner Manager uses ActiveMQ queues (set up and
configured by the user) between processing stages to add additional reliability to B2B message
processing. ActiveMQ destinations (queues) are automatically created when the first transaction is
processed through the runtime applications.

![A flowchart showing the architecture of a data processing system](_images/rtf-deployment.png)

## See Also

- [Deploying Partner Manager Runtime Apps](deploy-partner-manager)
- [Configuring and Managing Message Flows](message-flows)
---

# Message Flow Components

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

A message flow in Partner manager comprises configuration objects that execute end-to-end B2B
message processing:

- [Partner identifiers](partner-manager-identifiers)  
  Send and receive identifiers, such as X12 ISA and GS identifiers and AS2 identifiers
- [Receive and Send endpoints](endpoints)  
  Receive source messages or send transformed messages using a standard transport protocol
- [Source and target message types](partner-manager-create-message-type)  
  Settings that define the structures of received messages and of the transformed messages sent to
  the backend.
- [Maps](partner-manager-maps)  
  DataWeave translation maps that convert inbound EDI transactions to the back-end application
  format or transactions outbound from the backend to EDI format
---

# Creating Message Types

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can create new message types from the **<host>** page or by using the Message Flow wizard.

## Create a Message Type from the Host Page

To create a message type from the **<host>** page:

1.  In the sidebar, select **<host-name>**.
2.  From the **Message types** section, click **New**.
3.  In the **Usage** drop-down list, select the message usage.
4.  Add values to the remaining settings.

    For setting descriptions, see the topic that corresponds to the message type usage:
    - [Receive from Partners Message Type Settings](message-type-receive-from-partners)
    - [Send to Partners Message Type Settings](message-type-send-to-partners)
    - [Target at Host Message Type Settings](message-type-target-at-host)
    - [Source at Host Message Type Settings](message-type-source-at-host)

After you create Receive from Partners and Source at Host message types, you can add up to five
custom message attributes to them. For information, see
[Using Custom Message Attributes with Message Types](use-custom-attributes).

## Create a Message Type by Using the Message Flow Wizard

To create a message type by using the Message Flow Wizard, see
[Create Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow) and
[Create Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow).

## See Also

- [Modifying Message Type Settings](modify-message-type-settings)
- [Deleting Message Types](delete-message-types)
---

# Deleting Partners

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you are no longer trading with a partner, you can delete the partner.

When you delete a partner, the partner is removed from the Partner Managerâs **Partners** page. All
of the partnerâs data is deleted, except for partner activities that occurred before the partner was
deleted.

To delete a partner:

1.  From your Anypoint Partner Manager sandbox or production environment, select **Partners** from
    the sidebar.
2.  Select the partner to delete and click **Delete**.
3.  In the confirmation dialog, confirm the deletion.

After you delete a partner, you can reuse the partner name.

## See Also

- [Configuring and Managing Partners](configure-partner)
- [Creating Partners](create-partner)
- [Modifying Partner Settings](modify-partner-settings)
---

# Sender and Receiver Identifiers

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

AS2, EDIFACT, and X12 transactions use identifiers in messages to identify the senders and receivers
of the messages. For X12 messages, the identifiers in the ISA and GS headers provide valuable
information that enables identification of the appropriate flow to use to process the transaction.

Add identifiers and configure their values in the **<host>** page.

## Add and Configure a New Identifier

1.  Access the **<host> page**.
2.  In the **Identifiers** section, click **New**.
3.  In the **New Identifier** dialog, select the identifier type from the drop-down list:
    - [AS2 Identity](#as2-identifier)
    - [EDIFACT-UNB](#edifact-unb)
    - [Reference ID](#reference-id)
    - [X12 - GS](#x12-gs)
    - [X12 - ISA](#x12-isa)

4.  In **Identifier Value**, enter a value to uniquely name your identifier.
5.  If you selected **EDIFACT-UNB** or **X12-ISA** as the **Type** value, you must also select a
    **Qualifier** from the list.
6.  Click **Create identifier**.

## EDIFACT UNB Identifier Values

Use the following values to configure the EDIFACT UNB identifier type:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Qualifier</p></td><td><p>Select the qualifier types for the partnerâs EDIFACT UNB identifier from the drop-down menu.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Identifier value</p></td><td><p>Specify the EDIFACT-UNB ID for your partner, which can be up to 15 characters. Your partner supplies you with the proper EDIFACT Interchange Sender and Receiver IDs during onboarding.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## AS2 Identifier Values

To use AS2 transport to exchange B2B transactions with your partner, first configure the AS2
identifier for your organization and for your partner.

Use the following values to configure the AS2 identifier:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Identifier value</p></td><td><p>The AS2 identity for your partner. Your partner supplies you with the corresponding Sender and Receiver AS2 IDs during the onboarding process.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## X12 ISA Identifier Values

Use the following values to configure the X12 ISA identifier type:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Qualifier</p></td><td><div><div><p>Select the qualifier type for the partnerâs X12-ISA identifier from the drop-down menu.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Identifier value</p></td><td><p>EDI X12-ISA ID for your partner, which can be up to 15 characters. Your partner supplies you with the proper EDI X12 Interchange Sender and Receiver IDs during onboarding.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## X12 GS Identifier Values

Use the following values to configure the X12 GS identifier type:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Identifier value</p></td><td><p>EDI X12-GS ID for your partner, which can be up to 15 characters. Your partner supplies you with the proper EDI X12 Group Sender and Receiver IDs during onboarding.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Outbound Message Flow Partner Reference Identifiers

Outbound message flows that transform your back-end application messages to EDI format use the
partner Reference ID when sending data to your partners.

Unlike EDI transactions that have well-defined standards for how sender, receiver, and document type
information is presented in the data, the source messages for outbound B2B data exchange originating
from your enterprise back-end systems typically follow schemas. These schemas are usually defined by
your back-end application teams according to your organizationâs data models.

The receiving partner information about the application message from the back-end application might
not necessarily be the same as the name of the partner in Partner Manager. This scenario is similar
to how ISA and GS identifiers on an inbound EDI message might not be the same as the profile name of
the partner.

The Partner Manager outbound message processing framework makes a dynamic lookup to determine the
receiving partner based on the content in the application message received from the backend systems.

### Example

In this example, an outbound purchase order XML message from your ERP system to your partner,
Mythical Supplier, has the following content in the payload:

`<VendorName>MYTHICAL SUPPLIER, LLC</VendorName>`

An outbound payment remittance advice JSON message from your Financials system to the same partner
contains the following content in the payload:

`"PayeeName": "MYTHICAL ENTERPRISES"`

For Partner Manager to route these outbound transactions to the appropriate message flows for
Mythical Supplier, you must add these values as Partner Reference IDs from the partnerâs profile
page. To do this [create a new identifier](#create-identifier) and select the type as **Reference
ID**.

If your back-end application sends outbound transactions to partners on behalf of more than one
internal line of business, thus requiring you to set different X12 ISA and X12 GS sender identifiers
on the transformed outbound data (depending on which line of business is sending the transaction),
you can configure partner reference identifiers on your host profile, with the value in the
application messageâs payload that uniquely identifies the sending LoB.

The actual usage of partner reference identifiers and how outbound message routing is determined, is
explained in the outbound message documentation.

## See Also

- [Delete Identifiers](delete-identifiers)
- [Message Flows](message-flows)
- [Outbound Message Routing](outbound-message-routing)
---

# Creating Translation Maps

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Partner Manager uses DataWeave translation maps to transform business transactions between
your partnerâs message formats (CSV, EDIFACT, X12, JSON, or XML) and your organizationâs enterprise
application message formats (CSV, JSON, or XML).

To use a map, create the map in Anypoint Studio and then import the map to the [Maps](maps) page in
Partner Manager.

To start with an example of a map, download and then customize the
[B2B/EDI: Order to Cash - Mapping Examples](https://mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.muleesb.modules/b2b-order-to-cash-mapping/minor/1.0/)
from Exchange. This example includes the following transformations for message flows:

- Inbound message flows:
  - X12 4010 850 to JSON
  - EDIFACT D97A to JSON
  - CSV to JSON
  - XML to JSON

- Outbound message flows:
  - JSON to X12 4010 855
  - JSON to EDIFACT D97A
  - JSON to CSV
  - JSON to XML

To customize this example:

1.  Open the `.jar` file in Studio.
2.  Change the source message type.
3.  Change the target message type.
4.  Map the input to the output.
5.  Test the map locally.
6.  Import the map to a Partner Manager message flow.

## Open the .jar File in Studio

To open the `.jar` file:

1.  Launch Studio.
2.  From the **File** menu, select **Import > Packaged mule application (.jar)** and click **Next**.
3.  Select the downloaded `.jar` file and click **Open**.
4.  Click **Finish**.

## Change the Source Message Type

To change the message type:

1.  Click the **Global Elements** tab at the base of the Studio canvas.
2.  From **Global Configuration Elements**, select the configuration for the map to use in an
    inbound or outbound message flow.
3.  Click **Edit**.
4.  In **Name**, change the name of the configuration to something meaningful.
5.  If the message flow uses the EDI, JSON, X12, or XML format:
    1.  In **Schema definitions**, select **Edit inline**.
    2.  In **Value**, enter the version and transaction set to map to or from.

        For example, set **Value** to `/x12/005010/830.esl` to map to or from a `005010-830`
        transaction.

6.  Click **OK**.

## Change the Target Message Type

To change the target message type:

1.  If your target message type has an associated schema, copy the schema file into the
    `<Project-name>/src/main/resources` folder in the project.
2.  Select the **Transform Message** component.

    The new transaction set structure displays in one pane of the Transform Message graphical view,
    and your application message structure (CSV, JSON, or XML) displays in the other pane of this
    view.

3.  Map the input fields to the output fields.

## Test the Map Locally

To test the map locally:

1.  In **Package Explorer**, right-click on the template project, and select **Run > Run as Mule
    application**.
2.  Use a REST client to post a message payload.

    For example Postman calls, see the `B2B-Order-to-Cash-Mapping-Studio.postman_collection.json`
    file in the `examples` folder.

    The application returns the transformed payload.

3.  After you verify that the map works correctly, copy the DataWeave map from `src/main/resources`
    and rename it to something meaningful.

## Select the Map for a Partner Manager Message Flow

When you configure a message flow, you can select the `.dwl` file when adding the map from the
**Maps** page or on the **Select a map** page of the message flow. You can also
[version the map](maps#version-a-map) and change the map selection on a deployed message flow.

## See Also

- [Create Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow)
- [Create Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow)
- [DataWeave Quickstart](../../dataweave/latest/dataweave-quickstart)
- [Adding and Versioning Maps](maps)
---

# Anypoint Partner Manager Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

These release notes are provided for Anypoint Partner Manager:

- [Anypoint Partner Manager Release Notes](../../release-notes/partner-manager/anypoint-partner-manager-2.0-release-notes)

## See Also

- [Anypoint Partner Manager for B2B Integration](./)
---

# Receive Endpoints

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Partner Manager receive endpoints have the following functions:

- Receive from Partners endpoints act as source endpoints in inbound message flows that receive B2B
  messages from partners either directly or via third-party connections.
- Source at Host endpoints act as source endpoints in outbound message flows that receive messages
  from backend applications.

Partner Manager supports the following formats for receive endpoints:

- [AS2 (Receive from Partners endpoints only)](endpoint-as2-receive)
- [FTP](endpoint-ftp-receive)
- [HTTP and HTTPS](endpoint-https-receive)
- [SFTP](endpoint-sftp-receive-target)

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
---

# Replaying Transmissions

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Transmissions can fail due to data validation, mapping, or connectivity issues. If a transmission
fails after an Anypoint Partner Manager endpoint secures it, you can replay the transmission.

You can also replay transmissions that processed successfully. You might want to do this if:

- The translated payload is missing a field.

  In this scenario, you can upload a new translation map and replay the original transmission so
  that it goes through the updated map.

- The target system or partner that received the original translated payloads lost data due to a
  system issue and requested that it be resent.

Replaying a transmission regenerates the transmission source and reprocesses the original
transmission as a new transmission. You can replay both inbound and outbound transmissions.

The following rules apply to replaying transmissions:

- You can replay any original transmission that has a status of `Failed` or `Delivered`, except for
  inbound transmissions that failed AS2 security validation.
- The maximum number of transmissions that you can replay in a single request is 100.
- Partner Manager links replayed transmission instances to the original transmission record.
- When a transmission is replayed, Partner Manager sets its transmission status to `Replaying`. Once
  the replay action completes, Partner Manager shows the date of the last replay.

  To check the status of a replayed transmission, refresh the **Activity** screen and locate the
  transmission.

- Partner Manager processes the transmission payload through the active version of the message flow.
  Therefore, the new transmission uses the current map and endpoint configurations for resolution
  and routing.

  If Partner Manager doesnât find an active message flow for the transmission or message, it returns
  an error.

- Partner Manager doesnât check for duplicate control numbers when it replays inbound transmissions.
- Partner Manager sends acknowledgments for replayed inbound transmissions based on the partner or
  message flow configurations at the time of replaying.

## Replay Transmissions from the Transmission List View

To replay one or more transmissions from the transmissions list view:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Activity**.
2.  Select **Transmissions**.
3.  To replay one transmission:
    1.  Locate the transmission.
    2.  On the transmission action menu (**â¦â**), click **Replay**.

4.  To replay multiple transactions:
    1.  Locate and then select the transmissions.
    2.  Click **Replay**.

## Replay a Transmission from the Transmission Details View

You can replay a transmission from the transmissions details view:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Activity**.
2.  Select **Transmissions**.
3.  Locate the transmission to replay.
4.  Click the transmission to display the transmission details view.
5.  Click **Replay**.

## View a Transmissionâs Replay History

The replay history for a transmission shows the date for each replay and provides links to the
replays.

To view a transmissionâs replay history:

1.  In the transmissions list view, locate the transmission for which you want to see the replay
    history.
2.  Click the transmission to bring up the transmission details view.
3.  Click the last replayed date to see the following information about all replays of the
    transmission:
    - ID of the repayed transmissions
    - Replay dates
    - Replay status (a green checkmark for successful replay and a red exclamation point for a
      failed replay)

## See Also

- [Tracking Transmissions](activity-tracking)
---

# Configuring Runtime Fabric Deployment Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can deploy Partner Manager runtime applications to an
[Anypoint Runtime Fabric](../../runtime-fabric/latest/) instance configured in your environment.

## Prerequisites for Deploying to Runtime Fabric

Before you deploy Partner Manager to Runtime Fabric, you must:

- Ensure that you have the required permissions for using Runtime Fabric.
- Create and configure the Runtime Fabric instance into which to deploy Partner Manager services.
  See [Setting Up Runtime Fabric](../../runtime-fabric/latest/setting-up-runtime-fabric).
  - If you already have an RTF instance, you can use it to deploy Partner Manager runtime
    applications.

- Configure Persistence Gateway in Runtime Fabric, as described in
  [Persistence Gateway](../../runtime-fabric/latest/persistence-gateway).

  Partner Manager uses a persistent Object Store via Persistence Gateway to store transaction
  processing information such as EDI control numbers and message routing information, which the
  Partner Manager runtime applications access.

- Set up an ActiveMQ (JMS) messaging broker and configure the connection settings while configuring
  the Partner Manager Runtime Fabric deployment settings.

  Partner Manager uses ActiveMQ to reliably send payloads between different stages when it processes
  B2B transactions. The Partner Manager deployment service automatically creates the required
  message queues in your ActiveMQ instance when you deploy message flows.

## Configure Settings for Deploying to Runtime Fabric

To configure settings for deploying Partner Manager to Runtime Fabric, access the business unit and
environment for which you want to deploy Partner Manager and then follow these steps:

1.  From the access management **Configure a Partner Manager environment** window, enter the
    following general settings:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Deployment Target Type</p></td><td><p>Target type for your Partner Manager deployment. Select <strong>Runtime Fabric</strong>.</p></td><td><p>CloudHub 1.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Runtime Fabric</p></td><td><p>Name of the Runtime Fabric instance to use as your deployment target.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Runtime Version</p></td><td><p>Mule runtime engine version to run your Runtime Fabric instance.</p></td><td><div><div><p>4.6.0</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">If you select Mule runtime 4.9.0 LTS using Java 17, test your component versions and SFTP connections that rely on insecure ciphers to make sure they work as expected with Java 17.</div></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min Mule Version override</p></td><td><p>Minimum Mule runtime version required to deploy the runtime applications.</p><p>Configuring the Min Mule version enables you to apply a <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/feature-flagging#configure-feature-flags-for-a-mule-application">feature flagging</a> mechanism for Partner Manager-deployed Mule runtime applications.</p><p>If you process cXML messages with &lt;DOCTYPE&gt; tags, you must set the <code>minMuleVersion</code> to 4.5.0 or higher. If youâre migrating from an earlier version, you must perform end-to-end regression testing on your existing workflows before changing the <code>minMuleVersion</code>.</p></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">If you select <code>None</code>, Partner Manager defaults to using <code>4.3.0</code> as the Min Mule version.</div></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>The ingress template used for Partner Manager application deployments. For more information about ingress templates, see <a href="../../runtime-fabric/latest/custom-ingress-configuration">Ingress Templates</a>.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">On the Partner Manager host page, the <strong>URL</strong> column of the <strong>Endpoints</strong> section shows a placeholder URL for RTF Ingress endpoints. Partner Manager doesnât have visibility into the RTF configuration.</div></div></div></td><td><p>Selected by Runtime Manager</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payload compression</p></td><td><p>Select not to compress the payload, always compress the payload, or adaptively compress the payload only when Anypoint MQ fails because the payload size exceeds a certain threshold.</p></td><td><p>None (not compressed)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anypoint MQ Region</p></td><td><p>Region where Anypoint MQ is deployed.<br><br>When users replay transmissions when Partner Manager is deployed to Runtime Fabric, Partner Manager sends the associated transmission IDs to an Anypoint MQ queue. The document processing service app running in your Runtime Fabric instance reads the transmission IDs from Anypoint MQ to start reprocessing the transmission within your runtime applications. No transaction payload is processed via Anypoint MQ.</p></td><td><p>US East (N. Virginia)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce deploying replicas across nodes</p></td><td><p>Whether Runtime Fabric enforces the deployment of Partner Manager application instances across different nodes.</p></td><td><p>Selected</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use persistent object store</p></td><td><p>Whether Partner Manager application instances must use a persistent object store. This value is always selected and you canât modify it.</p></td><td><p>Selected</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Einstein enabled</p></td><td><p>Enables Einstein features in Partner Manager, such as the <a href="activity-message-tracking#message-summaries">message summarization</a> feature for source and target payloads.</p></td><td><p>Not selected</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Select **Applications** and enter the following application settings:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Application Name Prefix</p></td><td><p>Prefix appended to the Partner Manager application names. Maximum length is five characters.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Each B2B endpoint</p></td><td><p>Reserved CPU space, CPU space limit, and memory capacity for each Partner Manager endpoint. Partner Manager deploys a generic Mule app to the runtime for each endpoint protocol and direction (receive or send) combination. For help with sizing, contact your MuleSoft Account Team.</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Reserved CPU</strong>: 0.08 vCPU</p></li><li><p><strong>CPU Limit</strong>: 0.5 vCPU</p></li><li><p><strong>Memory</strong>: 0.9 GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>B2B API Replication Service</p></td><td><div><div><p>Reserved CPU space, CPU space limit, and memory capacity for the Partner Manager B2B API Replication Service.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Reserved CPU</strong>: 0.1 vCPU</p></li><li><p><strong>CPU Limit</strong>: 0.5 vCPU</p></li><li><p><strong>Memory</strong>: 1 GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>B2B Document Processing Service</p></td><td><p>Reserved CPU space, CPU space limit, and memory capacity for the Partner Manager B2B Document Processing Service.</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Reserved CPU</strong>: 0.1 vCPU</p></li><li><p><strong>CPU Limit</strong>: 0.5 vCPU</p></li><li><p><strong>Memory</strong>: 1.2 GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

3.  Click **Save**.
4.  Select **JMS Connection** and enter the JMS connection settings:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>JMS Broker URL</p></td><td><p>URL of the JMS broker.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Username used to connect to the JMS broker.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Password used to connect to the JMS broker.</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  Click **Save**.

## See Also

[Deploying Partner Manager Runtime Apps](deploy-partner-manager)
---

# Send Endpoints

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Partner Manager send endpoints have the following functions:

- Send to Partners endpoints act as target endpoints in outbound message flows to which transformed
  messages are sent to partners either directly or via third-party connections.
- Target at Host endpoints act as target endpoints in inbound message flows to which transformed
  messages are sent to backend applications.

Partner Manager supports the following formats for send endpoints:

- [AS2 (Send to Partners endpoints only)](endpoint-as2-send)
- [FTP](endpoint-ftp-send)
- [HTTP and HTTPS](endpoint-https-send)
- [SFTP](endpoint-sftp-send)

## See Also

- [Endpoints](endpoints)
- [Creating Endpoints](create-endpoint)
---

# Configuring a Payload Storage API

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Partner Manager enables you to configure your own storage solution (for example, a
database, Amazon S3, or Azure Blob Storage) to store transmission content.

To use your own storage solution, you must implement an API that provides Partner Manager with a
standardized way to connect to your storage.

> [!NOTE] If you want the payload to be available from the Partner Manager Activity page, the
> payload storage API must be publicly accessible. If your payload storage API is accessible only
> through your network, you must implement the API differently. See
> [Payload view URL](#payload-view) for more information.

## Download and Deploy the Payload Storage API

Before you create your first partner and message flow, you must configure your payload storage API
within the host organization in Partner Manager:

1.  Access and download the following APIs from Anypoint Exchange:
    - [Partner Manager Content Storage API](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.b2b/partner-manager-content-storage-api)

      RAML specification for the payload storage API, which includes the URI parameters and record
      ID (contentRecordId) that the API implementationâs HTTP response header must return

    - [Partner Manager Content Storage API - S3 Template](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.b2b/partner-manager-content-storage-service-s3)

      Sample template for creating the payload storage API using Amazon S3

2.  [Implement the API](../../general/api-led-develop) to connect with your choice of storage
    location; for example, a database, Amazon S3, or filesystem.
3.  [Deploy the API](../../runtime-manager/deployment-strategies) on a runtime that can access the
    storage solution.
4.  Make a note of the API URL and configuration values:
    - Host, port, and path connection
    - Connection Idle and Response timeouts
    - Authentication types:
      - None
      - API Key
      - Basic
      - Client Credentials
      - OAuth Token

    - Certificate (`.PEM` format) and connection types

      Youâll need this information when you associate Anypoint Partner Manager with your payload
      storage API. Your payload storage API must be accessible over the internet via HTTPS. Ensure
      you implement the appropriate authentication mechanisms to enable access over HTTPS.

## Add the Payload Storage API to the Host Profile

1.  In Anypoint Platform, open Anypoint Partner Manager.
2.  From the sidebar, select your organizationâs host page.
3.  In the **Payload storage** section of the page, click **Edit** to add the path to your payload
    storage.
4.  Enter the following values:
    - **Endpoint name**: The name of the payload storage API
    - **Description**: The optional description for your payload storage
    - **URL**: The path to your payload storage

      This URL does the following:
      - Stores payloads when transactions process in the runtime
      - Retrieves the payload when you click **View Payload** on the **Transaction Activity** page,
        unless a payload view URL is configured

    - **Payload view URL**: (Optional) A secondary payload storage API URL that you can use to view
      the payload on the **Activity** page if the payload storage API is restricted to your internal
      network. See the Partner Manager
      [API implementation](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.muleesb.modules/apm-payload-storage-with-ext-ui-template/minor/1.0/)
      example in Exchange for more information.

      If the payload storage API _isnât_ restricted to your internal network, leave this field blank
      so that you can view the payload by using the **View Payload** button.

5.  Optionally, if you want to view (or allow your users to view) the raw payloads of the files you
    receive or send, select **Allow users to download payloads**.
6.  Click **Save**.

## Change the Payload Storage API Configuration

To change the payload storage API configuration:

1.  In the **Payload storage** section of the **Host** page, click **Edit**.
2.  Modify the field values that require changing.
3.  Click **Save**.
4.  To have the changes take effect, either redeploy an existing message flow or deploy a new
    message flow.

## See Also

- [Configure and Manage a Host Profile](configure-host)
---

# Setting up the Partner Manager Environment

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Setting up the Anypoint Partner Manager environment involves the following:

- Configuring Partner Manager deployment settings.

  You can deploy Partner Manager to either CloudHub or Anypoint Runtime Fabric, and you can
  configure separate deployment settings for each business group environment. Configure Partner
  Manager deployment settings in access management, as described in
  [Deploying Partner Manager Runtime Apps](deploy-partner-manager).

- Configuring a payload storage API.

  You must configure a payload storage API that provides Partner Manager with a standardized way to
  connect to your solution for storing transmission content. For more information, see
  [Configuring a Payload Storage API](setup-payload-storage-api).

- Uploading your own, self-signed certificates, if you want to use them to establish secure
  connections with endpoints.

  You can use CA-signed certificates instead of your own self-signed certificates.

  For more information, see [Certificate and Keystore Storage](certificates).
---

# Troubleshooting Anypoint Partner Manager

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Partner Manager relies on several different MuleSoft technologies including but not limited
to Mule, Anypoint Runtime Manager, secrets manager, and your infrastructure for B2B storage
payloads.

Because Anypoint Partner Manager is used with other technologies, troubleshooting an issue with
Partner Manager often involves troubleshooting these other technologies as well.

Look for solutions in the documentation about connectivity and related errors or use the
documentation pageâs navigation pane to locate troubleshooting information specific to either
Anypoint Partner Manager or its related technologies.

## See Also

- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# Cannot process B2B transactions successfully

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You discover that transactions are not being processed when you test the message flow or when your
partner sends you B2B messages.

## Causes

Any of the following can lead to this type of error:

- Your payload storage is not set up correctly or is not functioning properly.

If the payload storage is not functional, B2B messages are not processed.

- The message flow configuration is not aligned with the message being received from the partner.

Examples of incorrect message flow configuration include incorrect or missing identifiers, incorrect
message types, and so forth.

- The Mule servers that process your B2B integration workloads are not functioning properly.

For example, your servers can be inactive, the apps might not be functioning correctly, and so
forth.

## Solutions

Perform some verifications with your payload storage or flow configurations.

### Verify Payload Storage

Verify the appropriate configuration for the payload storage API.

If the payload storage app is functioning, verify that the storage source (for example, the S3
bucket, Azure Blob store, or database) that you use as the storage solution is up and functioning.

- Open Anypoint Runtime Manager to verify if the related payload storage app is active.
- Try restarting the app.
- Test your payload storage API configuration using a browser extension such as Postman on Google
  Chrome.

### Verify Flow Configurations

Verify that your message flow configuration is compatible with that of your partner.

1.  From within your Anypoint Partner Manager environment (either Sandbox or Production), select
    your <Hostname> profile.
2.  Select **Message Flows**.
3.  Search for your message flow by the partner name or the message type such as 850.
4.  After you locate the message flow, click on the message flow card to open it up.
5.  From the message flow configurator, verify that the following information is in accordance with
    your agreement with your partner:
    - The correct identifiers are associated with the partner.
    - The appropriate receive endpoint is configured in the flow.
    - The appropriate document type (such as X12, version - 4010, or message type - 850) is
      configured.
    - Verify that you have the appropriate acknowledgment configuration.
    - Verify that you have an appropriate DataWeave map in the transformation section.
    - Verify that you have configured an appropriate target, such as both the message type of the
      target and the associated endpoint configuration.

6.  If all of these look good, then ensure that the last deployment for this flow was successful by
    viewing the latest deployment history next to the flow.
7.  If you observe a Failed status, try to deploy the message flow to ensure all your configurations
    have persisted on the Mule apps.

> [!NOTE] It can take up to five minutes for your changes to take effect on the runtime.

### Verify B2B Integration Apps

Verify that your B2B integration apps are active.

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select Anypoint Runtime Manager and **Applications**.
2.  Verify that the B2B apps on Mule are active by looking for a status of `started`. The list of
    apps includes:
    - APIReplicationService
    - DocumentProcessingService
    - One or more inbound apps.

The names of these apps are prefaced with inbound-

- One or more outbound apps.

The names of these apps are prefaced with outbound-

- Possibly one or more acknowledgment apps.

The names of these apps are prefaced with ack-

For any of the B2B integration apps that do not have a started status:

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select Anypoint Runtime Manager and **Applications**.
2.  For each app that does not have started status, select its row in the table and select
    **Restart** from the drop-down menu in the upper right of the status area in the information
    panel.
3.  If restarting the app does not help you recover the application, select its row in the table and
    select **Delete** from the drop-down menu in the upper right of the status area in the
    information panel and confirm the deletion.
4.  Navigate back to the message flow related to the app you just deleted and deploy the message
    flow.

Consider the following when performing a delete:

- If you delete the `DocumentProcessingService` or the `APIReplicationService`, deploying any flow
  results in the redeployment of those apps.
- If you delete any of the inbound, outbound, or ack apps, you must redeploy the specific flow
  corresponding to the deleted apps.

If you canât access the Support Portal, contact your Customer Success Manager or MuleSoft Account
Management team for help.
---

# Troubleshooting Configuration and Deployment-Related Errors

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you encounter errors as you set up your partners and message flows, the messages and solutions
described here can help you resolve your issue.
---

# Troubleshooting Connectivity or System-Related Errors

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you have problems receiving or sending data, the messages and solutions described here can help
resolve your issue.
---

# Troubleshooting Data Processing-Related Errors

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you encounter problems with message validations and transformations, the messages and solutions
described here can help you resolve your issue.
---

# Updated message flow configuration does not take effect on the runtime B2B integration applications

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

As you continue to use Anypoint Partner Manager, you might need to update the configuration
associated with a previously deployed message flow. For example, after updating the configuration,
you still find your message flow executing with the previous or older set of configurations.

## Causes

Any of the following can lead to this type of error:

- You might have not deployed the message flow after making changes to the message flowâs
  configuration.
- You might be within the five-minute synchronization window for changes from Anypoint Partner
  Manager to reflect on your B2B integration applications.

## Solutions

You can deploy the changed message flow or wait for the synchronization window to complete.

### Deploy the Changed Message Flow

After you make changes to the message flow configuration, you must deploy it in order for the new
configurations to become effective on your B2B integration applications.

To deploy your message flow after making changes:

1.  From within your Anypoint Partner Manager environment (either Sandbox or Production), select
    your <Hostname> profile.
2.  Select **Message Flows**.
3.  Search for your message flow by name.
4.  After you locate the message flow, click on the message flow card to open it.
5.  Confirm that each section in the flow shown in the interface displays a green checkmark next to
    it, and click Deploy to deploy the configurations to Mule.
6.  Provide the necessary reason to trigger a new deployment, and wait for the process to complete.

When the deployment is successfully completed, Anypoint partner manager responds with a successful
notification.

### Wait for the Synchronization Window to Complete for All Changes

Your runtime B2B integration applications executing on your Mule runtimes receive the latest
configuration from the Anypoint Partner Manager control plane at five-minute intervals.

Depending on when you made the configuration changes to your message flows in Partner Manager and
deployed them, your changes can take up to five minutes to take effect on the runtime.
---

# Failed to Find Deployed Message Flow Configurations

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This error sometimes occurs when you try to find a deployed message flow on the Activity page in
Anypoint Partner Manager:

```text
Failed to find deployed message flow configurations.
```

## Cause

The system is still processing the deployment of the message flow.

## Solution

Wait for the deployment to complete. This process can take up to 15 minutes.
---

# Failed to deploy: You cannot deploy this message flow because there is not enough capacity in this environment to deploy this message flow.

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Cause

You do not have sufficient vCore capacity to deploy the applications that support the message flow.

## Solution

Contact your MuleSoft Platform Administrator to review the vCore allocation for your business group.

If vCores are added, wait at least 15 minutes before attempting deployment.
---

# `Failure to store message payload`

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You might occasionally see tracking information such as this in the Activity section of Anypoint
Partner Manager:

```xml
Unrecognized standard from unrecognized partner
Received <datetime>  `Failure to store message payload`
File <name>
```

## Causes

This information appears in the Activity section when one of the following occurs:

- The payload storage API on the hostâs profile is not appropriately configured.
- The payload storage API is not functioning.
- The storage solution associated with the payload storage API is not functioning.

## Solution

Take the following actions to verify the Payload Storage API configuration:

1.  Verify the appropriate configuration for the payload storage API by performing a simple test
    request using a browser extension such as Postman on Google Chrome.
2.  Visit Anypoint Runtime Manager to verify that the related payload storage API is active and
    functioning by restarting the app.
3.  If the payload storage app is functioning, verify that the storage solution you are using (such
    as the S3 bucket, Azure Blob store, database, and so on) is up and functioning.

If all these scenarios have been verified successfully, and you still cannot deploy message flows,
please raise a case on the MuleSoft Support Portal.

If you have an existing commercial relationship with MuleSoft and are experiencing problems
accessing your Anypoint Platform account, you can open a case at
[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com/s/support). If you canât access the Support Portal,
contact your Customer Success Manager or MuleSoft Account Management team for assistance.
---

# Deployed Message flow configuration not found for Message: <name>

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You might occasionally see tracking information such as this in the Activity section of Anypoint
Partner Manager:

```xml
X12 from <partner>
Received <date>
File <name>
control #
TA1 sent
```

## Causes

This information appears in the Activity section when one of the following occurs:

- A transmission is received that contains a message type that is not configured for any partner or
  flow.
- A transmission is received that contains a message type that is not supported by Anypoint Partner
  Manager.

## Solution

Verify that the message type was received via the transmission:

1.  For the activity that you are viewing, inspect the message to determine the message type
    received. The message should contain a message identifier string like this:
    `v004010-810-1548-000000001`. Parts of this message are:
    - v004010: X12 format version received in a transmission
    - 810: Message type received
    - 1548: Group Segment Identifier (GS06)
    - 000000001: Message Segment Identifier (ST02)

2.  From within your Anypoint Partner Manager, Sandbox or Production environment select **Message
    Flows**.
3.  If you know the specific flow you are searching for, click on the card related to that flow to
    expand the associated configurations.
4.  If you do not know which flow you are looking for, search by partner name or message type (such
    as 850) to identify possible related message flows, and click on the message flow card to open
    up the flow.
5.  In the message flow configurator, verify that the appropriate message type (such as X12,
    version - 4010, message type - 850) is configured and that this matches the type of file that
    was listed in the Activity that indicated that an unrecognized standard was being used.
6.  If there is a discrepancy between the type of message configured in the message flow compared to
    the message received in the activity, then either contact your partner and ask them to send the
    appropriate file, or correct your message flow to ensure that the message type configured
    matches the type you expect to receive from your partner.

Partner Manager supports receiving only X12 files. If your partner sends you any other type of file
such as (JSON, XML, CSV, and so on), contact your partner and ask them to send the appropriate file
type to you.
---

# Missing TA1 acknowledgements for my transmissions

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Partner Manager sends TA1 acknowledgments for a received transmission if configured to do
so. Your partner might contact you to tell you that they are not receiving TA1 acknowledgments.

## Causes

Any of the following can lead to this type of error:

- The X12 settings for your partners are not configured to send TA1âs back to the partner.
- The endpoint to which you are sending that TA1âs back may be incorrectly configured.

## Solution

If you are not receiving TA1 configurations, review the following configuration:

1.  From within your Anypoint Partner Manager environment (either Sandbox or Production), select
    **Partners** and **Receive from <Partner_Name>**.
