2.  Scroll to Validation and Acknowledgment settings and click **X12**.
3.  Within the Acknowledgments section, ensure that the option for _Send TA1 for each transmission
    from <partner name>_ is selected.
4.  Configure the acknowledgment endpoint as in [Endpoints](endpoints).
5.  Click **Save** when done.

This enables TA1 acknowledgments for all transmissions received by the specific partner.
---

# You cannot deploy this message flow because the payload storage is not properly configured

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Cause

You have not configured the Payload Storage.

## Solutions

If your payload storage is configured, verify that it is active:

1.  From within Anypoint Partner Manager, select your `hostname` profile.
2.  Scroll down to Payload storage and click **Edit**.
3.  Review your payload storage configuration and status.
4.  If your payload storage is not configured, configure it as described in
    [Configuring a Payload Storage API](setup-payload-storage-api).
---

# `Unrecognized partner` transaction reported

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You might occasionally see tracking information such as this in the Activity section of Anypoint
Partner Manager:

```xml
<identifier> from Unrecognized partner
<date> control #
Partner not found for <identifier> with <value>
```

## Causes

This information appears in the Activity section when one of the following occurs:

- Partner Manager cannot identify the sender of the transmission either because the partner is not
  configured in Partner Manager, or because the identifier associated with the partner is not
  configured.
- Partner Manager cannot identify the receiver for the transmission because the identifier
  associated with the receiver is not configured.
- Partner Manager cannot identify both the sender and the receiver of this transmission.

## Solutions

Verify partner and host identifiers.

### Verify Partnerâs Identifiers

Verify that your partner has the appropriate identifiers configured as needed to support the
transmission.

1.  From within your Anypoint Partner Manager, Sandbox or Production environment select **Partners**
    <name of your specific partner related to this transmission>.
2.  From the Partners profile, scroll to the Identifiers section and verify that the identifier you
    are looking for is present in the table.
3.  If the desired partner identifier is not present, click **New** and select an identifier type
    from the Type list to uniquely identify your partner.
4.  Provide a value for your identifier (and a qualifier for X12-ISA) and click **Create
    identifier** to save your changes. See [X12 - ISA Identifier Values](x12-identity-settings) for
    more information about X12-ISA settings.

### Verify Organization (Host) Identifiers

Verify that your organization (the host) has the appropriate identifiers configured as needed to
support the transmission.

1.  From within your Anypoint Partner Manager, Sandbox or Production environment select **Partners**
    <name of your organizations / sub-organizations related to this transmission>.
2.  Within the Hostâs profile page, scroll to the Identifiers section and verify that the identifier
    you are looking for is present in the table.
3.  If the desired host identifier is not present, click **New** and select an identifier type from
    the Type list to uniquely identify your partner.
4.  Provide a value for your identifier (and a qualifier for X12-ISA) and click **Create
    identifier** to save your changes. See [X12 - ISA Identifier Values](x12-identity-settings) for
    more information about X12-ISA settings.
---

# `Unrecognized standard from unrecognized partner`

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You might occasionally see tracking information such as this in the Activity section of Anypoint
Partner Manager:

```xml
Unrecognized standard from unrecognized partner
Received <date>
File <name>
```

## Causes

This information appears in the Activity section when one of the following occurs:

- Partner Manager receives a transmission that is in an unsupported data format: for example,
  receiving a PDF, image, and so forth on receiving endpoints.
- Your partner sends you a valid X12 file but you have not configured a message flow that can accept
  it.
- You do not recognize the partner because you did not set them up in Anypoint Partner Manager.

## Solution

If you encounter any of these situations, verify that your partner is sending valid X12-formatted
files that align with the message flows that you associated with that partner.
---

# Undeploying Message Flows

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Undeploying a message flow prevents future messages from being processed and enables you to recover
resources that are consumed by a message flow that you no longer need.

You can undeploy your message flow from either a sandbox or a production environment. The
undeployment process takes about five minutes, during which actions such as `Deploy` and `Delete`
are unavailable.

> [!IMPORTANT] During the undeployment process, do not log out of Anypoint Partner Manager or close
> your browser window.

To undeploy a message flow:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Message Flows**.
2.  Select the message flow to undeploy from the list and click **Undeploy**.

Partner Manager checks to see if any of the applications deployed to Mule are orphaned as a result
of the undeployment. Partner Manager then cleans up orphaned applications to release any resources
that the applications consumed.

## See Also

- [Deploying and Testing Message Flows](deploy-message-flows)
- [Creating Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow)
---

# Upgrading Message Flow Runtime Templates

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Partner Manager uses prebuilt templates to convert your B2B message flow configurations
into generic runtime applications. As these templates are updated, you can upgrade your message
flows to use them to take advantage of new capabilities that enable better integration with your
partnerâs ecosystem.

Runtime templates related to Partner Manager are upgraded when:

- A new feature or functionality is added
- A component on which your environment is dependent, such as a connector version, is upgraded
- A security vulnerability is fixed
- Functional and performance defects are resolved in the template
- Flow optimizations are implemented

When new runtime templates are available, a tooltip shows on the **Manage deployments** button to
indicate thereâs a new version available.

To take advantage of new features, click **Manage deployments**, select **Upgrade deployments**,
confirm that you understand the effects of these actions, and then click **Confirm**.

Upgrading deployments updates all the Mule applications in your runtime with the latest template
versions that contain new features or bug fixes.

The process of updating all your message flows can take several minutes, depending on the number of
message flow configurations and runtime applications in your environment. Your existing message
flows continue uninterrupted during the upgrade process.

Before upgrading your production environment, upgrade your nonproduction environments to test the
changes on some of your message flows.

## See Also

- [Deploying and Testing Message Flows](deploy-message-flows)
---

# Using Custom Message Attributes with Message Types

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can associate up to five custom message attributes with a Receive from Partners or Source at
Host message type. Using these attributes enables you to have visibility into the full lifecycle of
your B2B workflow.

For example, you can define attributes for a messageâs PO number, Sales order number, Invoice
number, and Shipment ID. When you associate these attributes with a message type in a message flow,
Partner Manager extracts this data from the inbound or outbound payload and displays it in the
message view of the **Activity** window. From there, you can search for an attribute value, such as
a specific PO Number, so you can view every message that has the specified attribute value.

## Create a Custom Message Attribute

Use the **<host>** page to create new custom message attributes:

1.  In the sidebar, select **<host>**.
2.  From the **Custom message attributes** section, click **New**.
3.  Enter the attribute alias and label.

    The alias is used in the reference map that is associated with the message type. The label is
    used in the message view of the transactions. Both the alias and the label must be unique across
    the host.

4.  To make the attribute searchable on the **Activity** window, select the **Is searchable**
    checkbox.
5.  Repeat these steps for each new attribute you want to define.
6.  Click **Save**.

## Associate a Custom Message Attribute with a Message Type

To associate a custom message attribute with a message type, include definitions for those
attributes in the message typeâs reference map. For more information, see
[Creating Reference Maps for Message Types](create-reference-map).

## Dissociate a Custom Message Attribute from a Message Type

To dissociate a custom message attribute from a message type, remove it from the message typeâs
reference map.

## Delete a Custom Message Attribute

You can delete custom attributes that are not used in reference maps:

1.  In the sidebar, select the **<host-name>**.
2.  In the **Custom message attributes** section, locate the attribute you want to delete.
3.  In the row containing the attribute you want to delete, hover over the attribute label, select
    the trash can that appears, and click **Delete**.

## See Also

- [Creating Message Types](partner-manager-create-message-type)
- [Modifying Message Type Settings](modify-message-type-settings)
---

# X12 - ISA Identifier Values

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Refer to this information specifically for using ISA with X12.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Qualifier</p></td><td><div><div><p>The following Qualifier types are available with Anypoint Partner Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>01 (DUNS Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>02 (SCAC - Standard Carrier Alpha Code)</code></p></li><li><p><code>03 (FMC - Federal Maritime Commission)</code></p></li><li><p><code>04 (IATA - International Air Transport Association)</code></p></li><li><p><code>07 (Global Location Number (GLN))</code></p></li><li><p><code>08 (UCC EDI Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>09 (X.121)</code></p></li><li><p><code>10 (DoD - Department of Defense Activity Address Code)</code></p></li><li><p><code>11 (DEA - Drug Enforcement Administration)</code></p></li><li><p><code>12 (Phone Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>13 (UCS Code)</code></p></li><li><p><code>14 (DUNS Number and Suffix Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>15 (Petroleum Accountants Society of Canada Company Code)</code></p></li><li><p><code>16 (DUNS Number with a Four Character Suffix)</code></p></li><li><p><code>17 (American Bankers Association (ABA) Transit Routing Number (Including Check Digit, 9 Digit))</code></p></li><li><p><code>18 (Association of American Railroads (AAR) Standard Distribution Code)</code></p></li><li><p><code>19 (EDI Council of Australia Communications ID)</code></p></li><li><p><code>20 (HIN - Health Industry Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>21 (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System, or (IPEDS))</code></p></li><li><p><code>22 (Federal Interagency Commission on Education, or FICE)</code></p></li><li><p><code>23 (National Center for Education Statistics Common Core of Data 12-Digit Number for Pre-K-Grade 12 Institutes, or NCES)</code></p></li><li><p><code>24 (The College Boardâs Admission Testing Program 4-Digit Code of Postsecondary Institutes, or ATP)</code></p></li><li><p><code>25 (ACT, Inc. 4-Digit Code of Postsecondary Institutions.)</code></p></li><li><p><code>26 (Statistics of Canada List of Postsecondary Institutions)</code></p></li><li><p><code>27 (Carrier Identification Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>28 (Fiscal Intermediary Identification Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>29 (Medicare Provider and Supplier Identification Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>30 (U.S. Federal Tax Identification Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>31 (Jurisdiction Identification Number Plus 4)</code></p></li><li><p><code>32 (U.S. Federal Employer Identification Number (FEIN))</code></p></li><li><p><code>33 (National Association of Insurance Commissioners Company Code (NAIC))</code></p></li><li><p><code>34 (Medicaid Provider and Supplier Identification Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>35 (Statistics Canada Canadian College Student Information System Institution Codes)</code></p></li><li><p><code>36 (Statistics Canada University Student Information System Institution Codes)</code></p></li><li><p><code>37 (Society of Property Information Compilers and Analysts)</code></p></li><li><p><code>38 (The College Board and ACT, Inc. 6-Digit Code List of Secondary Institutions)</code></p></li><li><p><code>AM (Association Mexicana del Codigo de Producto (AMECOP) Communication ID)</code></p></li><li><p><code>NR (NRMA - National Retail Merchants Association)</code></p></li><li><p><code>SA (User Identification Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>SN (Standard Address Number)</code></p></li><li><p><code>ZZ (Mutually Defined)</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Identifier value</p></td><td><p>EDI X12-ISA ID for your partner; can be up to 15 characters. Your partner should supply you with the proper EDI X12 Interchange Sender and Receiver IDs.</p></td><td><p>Yes<br></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## X12 - GS Values

Refer to this information specifically for using GS with X12.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Identifier Value</p></td><td><p>EDI X12-GS ID for your partner; can be up to 15 characters. Your partner should supply you with the proper EDI X12 Group Sender/Receiver ID.</p></td><td><p>Yes<br></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## DUNS

Refer to this information specifically for using the DUNS number as an identifier.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Identifier Value</p></td><td><p>The DUNS number for your partner. The DUNS number is a nine-digit number that your partner supplies that corresponds to the Sender/Receiver ID.</p></td><td><p>Yes<br></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## AS2 Identity

Refer to this information specifically for using the AS2 identity to identify a partner.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Identifier Value</p></td><td><p>The AS2 identity for your partner. Your partner should supply you with the proper Sender/Receiver AS2 identity.</p></td><td><p>Yes<br></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete Identifiers

You might want to delete an identifier when it is no longer needed. Deleting an existing host or
partner identifier impacts the identification and routing of B2B messages to the associated partner.
Remember that there must always be at least one identifier per message flow.

Ask yourself the following when deleting an identifier: Why do you want to delete it? Do you need to
replace it with another identifier?

After successfully deleting an identifier, you can create a new identifier with the same value as
the one you deleted. If you need to replace the identifier you are deleting, record the values and
qualifier type needed.

1.  In the sidebar, select **Partners**.
2.  In the **Identifiers** section, hover over the identifier that you want to delete and click the
    trash can icon that appears in the right-hand side of the row.
3.  If you need a replacement identifier, you can add it now by clicking **New**.

## See Also

- [X12 Settings - Receive from Partner](x12-receive-read-settings)
---

# Configuring Receive Settings for Inbound X12 Messages

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Partner Manager enables you to configure the X12 acknowledegments, validation rules, character
encoding, and control numbers to apply when receiving inbound X12 messages from the selected
partner.

To configure X12 receive settings:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Partners**.
2.  Select the partner to associate with the settings.
3.  In the **Validation and acknowledgement settings** sectionâs **Receive from <partner-name>**
    column, click **X12**.
4.  Select a link to configure:
    - [Acknowledgment settings](#ack-settings)
    - [Validation settings](#validation-settings)
    - [Control numbers](#control-numbers)
    - [Character set and separators](#character-set)

5.  Click **Save**.

> [!NOTE] It can take up to about five minutes for your configuration changes to sync with Mule.

## Acknowledgment Settings

Use the following settings to configure the acknowledgments to send in response to inbound X12
messages:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Report non-fatal errors in acknowledgements</p></td><td><p>When checked, the AK3 segment of the generated 997 functional acknowledgment reports non-fatal errors.</p><p>When unchecked, the AK3 segment of the generated 997 functional acknowledgment doesnât report non-fatal errors.</p></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Report segment details</p></td><td></td><td><p>If selected, the details of segment errors are included in the functional acknowledgements generated (997) â¢</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enabled</p></td><td><p>Send TA1 for each transmission from &lt;recipient&gt;.</p></td><td><div><div><p>Provides a Technical Acknowledgment (TA1) to the Interchange sender that reports the status of the processing of an interchange header and trailer by the address receiver:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>If the ISA and IEA of a received X12 message are valid, the host sends a positive TA1 ACK, regardless of the status of the other content.</p></li><li><p>If the ISA and IEA of a received X12 message are not valid, the host sends a TA1 ACK with an error code.</p></li></ul></div><div><p>The TA1 ACK is sent inside an ISA or IEA envelope. The ISA and IEA are no different from any other interchange.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disabled</p></td><td><p>Send 997 functional acknowledgment when messages fail to route to a message flow</p></td><td><div><div><p>Sends a functional acknowledgment (the receipt and syntactical acceptability of a message) when there is a failure to route the message to one of the message flows.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### X12 997 Functional Acknowledgment

The X12 997 functional acknowledgment reports the status of a received interchange. It reports all
of the errors encountered while processing the received document. As with all X12 transaction sets,
the 997 functional acknowledgment is sent inside a GS or GE envelope.

The 997 functional acknowledgment does the following:

- Acknowledges the receipt of an interchange or functional group
- Accepts or rejects one or more functional groups or transactions
- Verifies and reports compliance with the standard

If an interchange contains multiple groups, a 997 acknowledgment is returned for each group.

If a group contains multiple transaction sets, then the acknowledgment for that group contains one
loop for each transaction set. This applies only if AK2 loops are generated for accepted transaction
sets.

## Validation Settings

Use these settings to ensure that all X12 messages received from a partner meet validation rules.
Partner Manager logs all validation errors and reports them in the CONTRL acknowledgements.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fail when value length outside allowed range</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects transactions with data element values that are too long or too short against the definition in the EDI schema used.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager uses the values.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Truncate values exceeding the length limits</p></td><td><p>If selected and <strong>Fail when value length outside allowed range</strong> isnât selected, Partner Manager truncates the data elements that exceed the maximum length limit in the generated output.</p></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fail when invalid character in value</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects transactions with invalid characters.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, the characters are either passed through or substituted.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fail if value is repeated too many times</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects transactions with values that are repeated too many or too few times.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager accepts the values.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fail if unknown segments are used</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, the Partner Manager rejects transactions that contain unknown segments.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager ignores unused segments.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fail when segments are out of order</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects transactions that have out-of-order segments.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, the segments are reordered.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fail when too many repeats of segment</p></td><td><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects transactions with segments repeated too many times.</p></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fail when unused segments are included</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager rejects transactions containing segments marked as unused in the schema.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager ignores unused segments.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce conditional relationship validations</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, any missing conditional data elements cause the generation of the X12 transaction to fail.</p></li><li><p>If not selected, Partner Manager does not enforce relationship validations.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce code set validations</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, Partner Manager enforces X12 code set validations, as defined in the X12 schemas.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Acknowledge every transaction</p></td><td><p>If selected, Partner Manager includes a separate AK2/AK5 segment for every received transaction set, even when all transactions are accepted in the 997 functional acknowledgment.</p></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include trailer segment details</p></td><td><p>If selected, Partner Manager includes elements from the Interchange Trailer (IEA) and Group Trailer (GE) segments of the X12 data payload.</p></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore included segments</p></td><td><p>If selected, the X12 read operation output includes segments with usage type 'Ignore', and you can use the segment s in message flow maps.</p></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Control Numbers

Use these settings to apply validations related to the use of control numbers within inbound X12
messages:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Requires unique interchange control number (ISA13)</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, the host records the interchange numbers previously processed and rejects duplicate interchange numbers from the same partner (as determined by the interchange sender and receiver identification).</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Requires unique group control number (GS06)</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, the host enforces globally unique Group Control Numbers (GS06) for received functional groups.</p></div><div><p>This configuration requires group numbers to be unique across all interchanges received from the same partner and application, as determined by the interchange sender and receiver identification, combined with the functional group sender and receiver application codes.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Require unique transaction set control number (ST02)</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, the host enforces globally unique Transaction Set Control Numbers (ST02) for received transaction sets.</p></div><div><p>This configuration requires transaction set numbers to be unique across all functional groups received from the same partner and application, as determined by the interchange sender and receiver identification, combined with the functional group sender and receiver application codes.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Character Set and Encoding

Use these settings to apply character set and encoding options for received X12 message:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Character set</p></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the characters allowed in string data. When set, invalid characters are replaced by the substitution character. If no substitution character is enabled for receive messages in the parser options, Partner Manager rejects the messages. Either way, the invalid characters are logged and are reported in the 997 functional acknowledgements for the receive messages.</p></div></div></td><td><p><code>EXTENDED</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates the character encoding for messages.</p></div></div></td><td><p><code>ISO8859_1</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Creating Inbound Message Flows](create-inbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Partners](create-partner)
- [Modifying Partner Settings](modify-partner-settings)
---

# Configuring Send Settings for Outbound X12 Messages

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Partner Manager enables you to configure the X12 envelope parameters, delimiters, validation rules,
character encoding, and control numbers to apply when sending an outbound X12 transmission to a
partner.

To configure a partnerâs X12 send settings:

1.  In the sidebar, select **Partners**.
2.  Select the partner for whom you want to configure the settings.
3.  In the **Validation and acknowledgement settings** sectionâs **Send to <partner-name>** column,
    click **X12**.
4.  In the menu, select the setting to configure:
    - [General settings](#general-settings)
    - [Authorization settings](#authorization-settings)
    - [TA1 Acknowledgment settings](#ta1-ack-settings)
    - [Global control numbers](#global-control-numbers)
    - [Character set & separators](#character-set)

5.  Click **Save**.

The values you configure in the partnerâs settings page are used by default for all X12 messages
going to that partner.

You can override the settings you configure at the partner level except for TA1 and control numbers.
You can override the message-flow-level settings by selecting the **Custom** option while creating
an outbound message flow.

> [!NOTE] Your changes can take up to five minutes to take effect on the runtime.

## General Settings

Use the following settings to configure the transaction usage, specify the responsible standards
agency, and enforce length limits and conditional relationship validations:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Usage indicator (ISA15)</p></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates if the X12 transaction sent is for testing, production, or information only.</p></div><div><p>Possible values include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>I - Information</p></li><li><p>T - Test</p></li><li><p>P - Production</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><code>P - Production</code><br>For production environments</p></li><li><p><code>T - Test</code><br>For sandbox environments</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Responsible agency code (GS07)</p></td><td><div><div><p>Define the standards agency responsible for the standard in the GS segment.</p></div><div><p>Possible values include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>T - Transportation Data Coordinating Committee (TDCC)</p></li><li><p>X - Accredited Standards Committee X12</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p><code>X</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce length limits</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, any data elements with a length outside of the definition in the X12 schema cause the generation of the X12 transaction to fail.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce conditional relationship validations</p></td><td><p>If selected, any missing conditional data elements cause the generation of the X12 transaction to fail.</p></td><td><p>Enabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce code set validations</p></td><td><p>If selected, Partner Manager enforces X12 code set validations, as defined in the X12 schemas.</p></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Authorization Settings

Use the following settings to configure the authorization and security qualifier for the
transactions:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Authorization qualifier (ISA01)</p></td><td></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Security qualifier (ISA03)</p></td><td></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## TA1 Acknowledgment Settings

Use the following settings to configure whether a technical acknowledgment is required, and the
receive endpoint for the acknowledgment:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Expect TA1 for each transmission sent to &lt;partner&gt;</p></td><td><div><div><p>If selected, sets the value in ISA14 to 1 on the generated X12 payload, indicating that a technical acknowledgment (TA1) is required.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Disabled</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>When the option to expect a TA1 is selected, click <strong>Select</strong> to specify the <strong>Receive from &lt;partner&gt;</strong> endpoint. Partner Manager will receive the TA1 as an inbound transmission.</p></div></div></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Global Control Numbers

Partner Manager applies a global control number at the partner level on the X12 interchanges and
functional groups generated from outbound message flows. These control numbers are sequenced across
all message types (including functional acknowledgments) that are sent to a partner.

The control number starts with a default value of `1`, and is sequentially increased as your
organization begins sending outbound transmissions.

To set the control number sequence value to a different number to maintain sequencing and avoid
duplication:

1.  Click **Change** and enter the desired control number.
2.  Click **Save**.

> [!NOTE] Your changes can take up to five minutes to take effect on the runtime.

## Character Set & Separators

Use the following settings to set the character encoding and delimiters for the generated X12
payload:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Setting</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Character set</p></td><td><div><div><p>Set of characters allowed in string data.</p></div></div></td><td><p>EXTENDED</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Character encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>Character encoding for messages.</p></div></div></td><td><p>UTF8</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Segment terminator</p></td><td><div><div><p>Segment terminator character.</p></div></div></td><td><p><code>~</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Data element separator</p></td><td><div><div><p>Data element separator character (can be a hex code).</p></div></div></td><td><p><code>*</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Component element separator (ISA16)</p></td><td><div><div><p>Component element separator character (can be a hex code).</p></div></div></td><td><p><code>&gt;</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Line ending between segments</p></td><td><div><div><p>Character to use for line endings between segments to improve the readability of the output message text. Possible values include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p><div><p>Generates the EDIFACT payload as a single line with segments separated by this character</p></div></li><li><p>CR</p><div><p>Carriage return</p></div></li><li><p>CRLF</p><div><p>Carriage return and line feed</p></div></li><li><p>LF</p><div><p>Line Feed</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>CRLF</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use segment line ending in last line</p></td><td><p>If <code>Yes</code>, Partner Manager applies the segment line ending character to the last segment in the payload.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Creating Outbound Message Flows](create-outbound-message-flow)
- [Creating Partners](create-partner)
- [Modify Partner Settings](modify-partner-settings)
